words.definition: Used to indicate that everything has gone well or according to plan up to the present moment, though the situation is not yet complete.
words.definitionUz: Hali hamma narsa tugamagan bo'lsa-da, hozirga qadar hamma narsa yaxshi yoki reja bo'yicha ketayotganini bildirish uchun ishlatiladi.
words.example1: "How is the new project going?" "So far so good — we are ahead of schedule."
words.example1Translation: "Yangi loyiha qanday ketmoqda?" "Hozircha hamma narsa yaxshi — biz jadvaldan oldindamiz."
words.example2: The surgery has been going well — so far so good, but there is still more to do.
words.example2Translation: Operatsiya yaxshi ketmoqda — hozircha hamma narsa yaxshi, lekin hali qilish kerak bo'lgan narsalar bor.
words.example3: We have finished three of the five tasks, and so far so good.
words.example3Translation: Biz beshta vazifadan uchtasini tugatdik va hozircha hamma narsa yaxshi.
extraExamples: The team asked how the new employee was settling in and the manager said, "So far so good."
We have driven halfway through the desert — so far so good, no engine problems.
"Are the negotiations going well?" "So far so good, both sides are cooperating."
The diet started two weeks ago and so far so good — she has already lost three kilos.
The festival has been running smoothly — so far so good despite the earlier weather concerns.
He is learning to cook and so far so good — nothing has burned yet.
"How is your first week at university?" "So far so good — I love the classes."
extraExampleTranslations: Jamoa yangi xodimning moslashib ketayotgani haqida so'radi va menejer "hozircha hamma narsa yaxshi" dedi.
Biz cho'l bo'ylab yarmiga yetdik — hozircha hamma narsa yaxshi, dvigatel muammolari yo'q.
"Muzokaralar yaxshi ketmoqdami?" "Hozircha hamma narsa yaxshi — ikki tomon ham hamkorlik qilmoqda."
Parhezni ikki hafta oldin boshladi va hozircha hamma narsa yaxshi — u allaqachon uch kilogramm yo'qotdi.
Festival muammosiz o'tmoqda — oldingi ob-havo xavotirlariga qaramay hozircha hamma narsa yaxshi.
U pishirishni o'rganmoqda va hozircha hamma narsa yaxshi — hali hech narsa kuyib ketmadi.
"Universitetdagi birinchi haftang qanday?" "Hozircha hamma narsa yaxshi — darslarga qoyil qoldim."

words.definition: To attract someone's attention visually, often because something is interesting, attractive, or unusual.
words.definitionUz: Ko'pincha qiziqarli, chiroyli yoki g'ayrioddiy bo'lgani uchun kimningdir ko'zini tortmoq yoki diqqatini jalb qilmoq.
words.example1: A bright red dress in the shop window caught her eye immediately.
words.example1Translation: Do'kon derazasidagi qizil ko'ylak darhol uning e'tiborini tortdi.
words.example2: His unusual hairstyle caught everyone's eye as he walked into the room.
words.example2Translation: Xonaga kirib kelganida uning g'ayrioddiy soch turmagi hamma diqqatini tortdi.
words.example3: A small headline at the bottom of the page caught my eye while reading the newspaper.
words.example3Translation: Gazetani o'qiyotganda sahifaning pastidagi kichik sarlavha diqqatimni tortdi.
extraExamples: The colorful painting on the wall immediately caught my eye when I entered the gallery.
Something about his confident posture caught her eye across the crowded room.
A job advertisement in the magazine caught his eye and changed his career path.
The shop display was designed to catch the eye of passing customers.
Nothing in the menu particularly caught my eye, so I asked the waiter for a recommendation.
Her creative portfolio caught the eye of a senior designer at the firm.
A strange car parked outside caught the neighbor's eye early in the morning.
extraExampleTranslations: Galereya xonasiga kirganimda devordagi rang-barang rasm darhol e'tiborimni tortdi.
Gavjum xona bo'ylab uning ishonchli turishi nimasidir uni diqqatini tortdi.
Jurnaldagi ish e'loni uning diqqatini tortdi va uning martaba yo'nalishini o'zgartirdi.
Do'kon vitrinasi o'tib ketayotgan mijozlar diqqatini tortish uchun mo'ljallangan edi.
Menyudagi hech narsa diqqatimni ayniqsa tortmadi, shuning uchun ofitsiantdan tavsiya so'radim.
Uning ijodiy portfolio firmadagi katta dizayner diqqatini tortdi.
Tashqarida to'xtab turgan g'ayrioddiy mashina ertalab qo'shni diqqatini tortdi.

words.definition: The ability to sustain prolonged physical or mental effort, pain, or difficult conditions without giving up.
words.definitionUz: Uzoq davom etadigan jismoniy yoki ruhiy kuch-g'ayrat, og'riq yoki qiyin sharoitlarga taslim bo'lmasdan bardosh berish qobiliyati.
words.example1: Marathon runners need exceptional endurance to complete the race.
words.example1Translation: Marafon yuguruvchilari poygani yakunlash uchun g'ayritabiiy chidamlilikka ega bo'lishlari kerak.
words.example2: The soldiers were trained to build mental endurance as well as physical strength.
words.example2Translation: Askarlar jismoniy kuchga qo'shimcha ravishda ruhiy chidamlilikni mustahkamlash uchun o'qitildi.
words.example3: Her endurance during the illness inspired everyone around her.
words.example3Translation: Kasallik davrida uning chidamliligi atrofidagilarning barchasiga ilhom berdi.
extraExamples: Climbing the mountain tested the team's endurance to its limit.
Endurance training helps athletes perform consistently over long periods.
Working two jobs while raising children requires enormous endurance.
The endurance of the early explorers under extreme conditions was remarkable.
Swimming long distances builds both muscle strength and cardiovascular endurance.
His endurance in the face of repeated failure eventually led to great success.
Endurance sports like cycling and rowing are becoming increasingly popular.
extraExampleTranslations: Tog'ga chiqish jamoaning chidamliligini oxirigacha sinadi.
Chidamlilikni mashq qilish sportchilarga uzoq vaqt davomida izchil natijalar ko'rsatishga yordam beradi.
Bolalarni tarbiyalash bilan birga ikki joyda ishlash ulkan chidamlilikni talab qiladi.
Dastlabki tadqiqotchilarning ekstremal sharoitlardagi chidamliligi ajoyib edi.
Uzoq masofaga suzish ham mushak kuchini, ham yurak-qon tomir chidamliligini oshiradi.
Takroriy muvaffaqiyatsizlik yuzasida uning chidamliligi oxir-oqibat katta muvaffaqiyatga olib keldi.
Velosiped haydash va eshkak eshish kabi chidamlilik sporti tobora ommalashib bormoqda.

words.definition: To bring shame, dishonour, or embarrassment upon a person, group, or institution through unacceptable behaviour.
words.definitionUz: Qabul qilib bo'lmaydigan xatti-harakatlar orqali biror kishi, guruh yoki muassasaga sharmandalik, nomus yoki uyat keltirmoq.
words.example1: The corrupt official was told he was a disgrace to the entire government.
words.example1Translation: Poraxo'r mansabdor shaxsga uning butun hukumat uchun uyat ekanligi aytildi.
words.example2: His violent behaviour on the pitch was a disgrace to the sport.
words.example2Translation: Uning maydondagi zo'ravonlik xatti-harakati sport uchun sharmandali edi.
words.example3: Cheating in the exam was a disgrace to the school that had trusted him.
words.example3Translation: Imtihonda aldash unga ishongan maktab uchun sharmandali edi.
extraExamples: The soldier's cowardice in battle was considered a disgrace to his unit.
Stealing from the charity was a disgrace to the entire community.
He was told that his selfish attitude was a disgrace to the family name.
Leaving the elderly without support is a disgrace to any civilized society.
The manager's dishonest dealings were a disgrace to the profession.
Her rude behaviour toward the guests was a disgrace to the host family.
Abandoning your teammates in a crisis is a disgrace to the spirit of teamwork.
extraExampleTranslations: Askarning jangdagi qo'rqoqligi o'z bo'linmasi uchun sharmandalik deb hisoblandi.
Xayriya tashkilotidan o'g'irlik butun jamoa uchun sharmandali edi.
Unga uning xudbinona munosabati oila nomiga dog' tushirishi aytildi.
Keksalarni qo'llab-quvvatlashsiz qoldirish har qanday sivilizatsiyalashgan jamiyat uchun sharmandalikdir.
Menejerning halolsiz muomalalari kasb uchun uyat edi.
Uning mehmonlarga qo'pol muomalasi uy egasi oilasi uchun sharmandali edi.
Inqiroz paytida jamoangizni tashlab ketish jamoaviy ruhiyat uchun sharmandalikdir.

words.definition: A small or partial amount of accuracy or validity within a statement, claim, or story that is not entirely true.
words.definitionUz: To'liq haqiqat bo'lmagan gap, da'vo yoki hikoyada kichik yoki qisman to'g'rilik yoki asoslilik miqdori.
words.example1: The rumour was exaggerated, but there was an element of truth in it.
words.example1Translation: Mish-mish bo'rttirib yuborilgan edi, lekin unda bir oz haqiqat ulushi bor edi.
words.example2: His criticism was harsh, but most people agreed it contained an element of truth.
words.example2Translation: Uning tanqidi qattiq edi, lekin ko'pchilik unda bir oz haqiqat ulushi borligiga qo'shildi.
words.example3: The documentary was biased, but experts admitted it had an element of truth.
words.example3Translation: Hujjatli film bir yoqlama edi, lekin mutaxassislar unda bir oz haqiqat ulushi borligini tan oldilar.
extraExamples: Her story seemed unbelievable, but investigators found an element of truth in her account.
Every stereotype, however unfair, tends to contain an element of truth.
The gossip was mostly false, but there was an element of truth buried in it.
His exaggerated complaint still had an element of truth that management could not ignore.
The novel was fiction, but readers recognized an element of truth in the characters.
Critics dismissed the theory, though they admitted it had an element of truth.
Even his most dramatic statements usually contained an element of truth.
extraExampleTranslations: Uning hikoyasi ishonilmaydigan bo'lib tuyuldi, lekin tergovchilar uning bayonida bir oz haqiqat ulushini topdilar.
Har qanday klishe, qanchalik adolatsiz bo'lmasin, bir oz haqiqat ulushini o'z ichiga olishga moyil.
G'iybat asosan yolg'on edi, lekin uning ichida bir oz haqiqat ulushi yashiringan edi.
Uning bo'rttirilgan shikoyatida hali ham menejment e'tiborsiz qoldirolmaydigan bir oz haqiqat ulushi bor edi.
Roman badiiy asar edi, lekin o'quvchilar qahramonlarda bir oz haqiqat ulushini tanidilar.
Tanqidchilar nazariyani rad etishdi, garchi ular unda bir oz haqiqat ulushi borligini tan olgan bo'lsalar-da.
Hatto uning eng dramatik bayonotlarida ham odatda bir oz haqiqat ulushi bor edi.

words.definition: The quality of being careful and prudent in one's behaviour or speech, especially regarding sensitive information; also the freedom to make independent judgements.
words.definitionUz: Ayniqsa nozik ma'lumotlarga nisbatan o'z xatti-harakatlari yoki nutqida ehtiyotkor va aqlli bo'lish sifati; shuningdek, mustaqil hukm chiqarish erkinligi.
words.example1: The matter requires complete discretion — please do not share it with anyone.
words.example1Translation: Masala to'liq ehtiyotkorlikni talab qiladi — iltimos, buni hech kim bilan ulashmang.
words.example2: The manager handled the complaint with great discretion and professionalism.
words.example2Translation: Menejer shikoyatni katta ehtiyotkorlik va professionallik bilan ko'rib chiqdi.
words.example3: Doctors must exercise discretion when sharing a patient's personal health information.
words.example3Translation: Shifokorlar bemorning shaxsiy sog'liq ma'lumotlarini ulashishda ehtiyotkorlik bilan ish yuritishlari kerak.
extraExamples: Use your own discretion when deciding how much detail to include in the report.
The journalist showed discretion by not publishing the victim's name.
In sensitive negotiations, discretion is more valuable than speed.
She trusted him with the secret because he had always shown discretion before.
Staff members are expected to use discretion when dealing with personal client data.
The case was handled with the utmost discretion to protect the family's privacy.
He left the decision to the team's discretion, trusting them to choose wisely.
extraExampleTranslations: Hisobotga qancha tafsilot kiritishni hal qilishda o'z mulohazangizdan foydalaning.
Jurnalist jabrlanuvchining ismini nashr etmaslik orqali ehtiyotkorlik ko'rsatdi.
Nozik muzokaralarda ehtiyotkorlik tezlikdan qimmatroq.
U unga sirni ishonib topshirdi, chunki u doim ilgari ehtiyotkorlik ko'rsatgan edi.
Xodimlardan mijozlarning shaxsiy ma'lumotlari bilan ishlashda ehtiyotkorlik kutiladi.
Oilaning maxfiyligini himoya qilish uchun ish eng katta ehtiyotkorlik bilan ko'rib chiqildi.
U qarorni jamoaning mulohazasiga qoldirdi, ularning donolik bilan tanlashlariga ishonib.

words.definition: To actively participate in or take part in an activity, conversation, or process.
words.definitionUz: Biror faoliyat, suhbat yoki jarayonda faol ishtirok etmoq yoki shug'ullanmoq.
words.example1: Students are encouraged to engage in class discussions to improve their skills.
words.example1Translation: O'quvchilar ko'nikmalarini oshirish uchun sinf muhokamalarida ishtirok etishga rag'batlantiriladi.
words.example2: The two countries agreed to engage in diplomatic talks to resolve the dispute.
words.example2Translation: Ikki mamlakat nizoni hal qilish uchun diplomatik muzokaralarda ishtirok etishga kelishdi.
words.example3: He was known to engage in extreme sports during his holidays.
words.example3Translation: U ta'tillarida ekstremal sport bilan shug'ullanishi bilan mashhur edi.
extraExamples: It is important to engage in regular physical exercise for good health.
The company decided not to engage in activities that could harm the environment.
She chose to engage in volunteer work after finishing university.
Both sides need to engage in honest dialogue for peace to be possible.
Young people are more likely to engage in risky behavior without proper guidance.
The team engaged in a lengthy debate before reaching a final decision.
He refused to engage in gossip, which earned him the respect of his colleagues.
extraExampleTranslations: Yaxshi sog'liq uchun muntazam jismoniy mashqlarga shug'ullanish muhim.
Kompaniya atrof-muhitga zarar yetkazishi mumkin bo'lgan faoliyatlarda ishtirok etmaslikka qaror qildi.
U universitetni tugatgandan so'ng ko'ngillilik ishida ishtirok etishni tanladi.
Tinchlik mumkin bo'lishi uchun ikki tomon ham halol muloqotda ishtirok etishi kerak.
Yoshlar to'g'ri yo'l-yo'riqsiz xavfli xatti-harakatlarga ko'proq moyil bo'lishi mumkin.
Jamoa yakuniy qarorga kelishdan oldin uzoq muddatli munozarada ishtirok etdi.
U g'iybatga shug'ullanishdan bosh tortdi, bu hamkasblari hurmatini qozonishiga yordam berdi.

words.definition: To agree completely with someone on a particular matter or to share the same opinion or viewpoint.
words.definitionUz: Biror masalada biror kishi bilan to'liq kelishmoq yoki bir xil fikr yoki nuqtai nazarni baham ko'rmoq.
words.example1: The two managers rarely see eye to eye on budget decisions.
words.example1Translation: Ikki menejer byudjet qarorlari bo'yicha kamdan-kam hollarda bir fikrda bo'ladi.
words.example2: We do not always see eye to eye, but we respect each other's opinions.
words.example2Translation: Biz har doim ham bir fikrda bo'lmaymiz, lekin bir-birimizning fikrlarini hurmat qilamiz.
words.example3: After a long discussion, the team finally saw eye to eye on the new strategy.
words.example3Translation: Uzoq muhokamadan so'ng, jamoa nihoyat yangi strategiya bo'yicha bir fikrga keldi.
extraExamples: My father and I rarely see eye to eye on political matters.
The partners saw eye to eye on most things, which made the business run smoothly.
It is natural for siblings not to see eye to eye on everything.
The two countries finally saw eye to eye after months of difficult negotiations.
We do not see eye to eye on this issue, but we can still work together.
She and her boss finally saw eye to eye after a frank conversation.
Seeing eye to eye with your co-workers makes collaboration much easier.
extraExampleTranslations: Otam va men siyosiy masalalarda kamdan-kam hollarda bir fikrda bo'lamiz.
Hamkorlar ko'p narsalarda bir fikrda bo'lishdi, bu biznesning silliq yurishini ta'minladi.
Aka-ukalar yoki opa-singillarning hamma narsada bir fikrda bo'lmasligi tabiiy.
Ikki mamlakat oylab davom etgan qiyin muzokaralardan so'ng nihoyat bir fikrga keldi.
Biz bu masalada bir fikrda emasmiz, lekin baribir birgalikda ishlay olamiz.
U va uning rahbari ochiq suhbatdan keyin nihoyat bir fikrga keldilar.
Hamkasblaringiz bilan bir fikrda bo'lish hamkorlikni ancha osonlashtiradi.

words.definition: To deteriorate progressively in quality, condition, or performance; to decline steadily.
words.definitionUz: Sifat, holat yoki ishlashda asta-sekin yomonlashib bormoq yoki pasayib ketmoq.
words.example1: The restaurant used to be excellent, but the food quality has really gone downhill.
words.example1Translation: Restoran ilgari ajoyib edi, lekin taomlar sifati rostdan ham yomonlashib ketdi.
words.example2: After the new management took over, the company started to go downhill.
words.example2Translation: Yangi rahbariyat boshqaruvni o'z qo'liga olgandan keyin kompaniya yomonlasha boshladi.
words.example3: His health began to go downhill after years of ignoring medical advice.
words.example3Translation: Tibbiy maslahatlarga yillab e'tibor bermagandan so'ng uning sog'lig'i yomonlasha boshladi.
extraExamples: Their friendship started to go downhill after the argument over money.
Once the founder left, the startup quickly went downhill.
The neighborhood went downhill after the factory closed and unemployment rose.
His performance at work went downhill as his personal problems increased.
The team's results started going downhill in the second half of the season.
Once you stop exercising regularly, your fitness level can go downhill fast.
The relationship went downhill as soon as trust was broken between them.
extraExampleTranslations: Ularning do'stligi pul ustidagi janjal tufayli yomonlasha boshladi.
Asos soluvchi ketgandan so'ng, startup tezda yomonlashdi.
Zavod yopilishi va ishsizlik o'sgandan keyin mahalla yomonlashdi.
Shaxsiy muammolari ortishi bilan ishdagi natijalari yomonlashdi.
Jamoaning natijalari mavsumning ikkinchi yarmida pasaya boshladi.
Muntazam mashq qilishni to'xtatgandan so'ng, jismoniy shakllanish darajasi tezda pasayib ketishi mumkin.
Ular o'rtasida ishonch sindirilib, munosabatlar yomonlashdi.

words.definition: At or during an initial stage of a process, event, or period of time.
words.definitionUz: Biror jarayon, voqea yoki vaqt davrining boshlang'ich bosqichida yoki shu paytda.
words.example1: Early on in the project, the team realized they needed more resources.
words.example1Translation: Loyihaning dastlabki bosqichida jamoa ko'proq resurslarga ehtiyoj sezdi.
words.example2: Early on in her career, she worked long hours to prove herself.
words.example2Translation: Karerasining boshida u o'zini isbotlash uchun uzoq soatlab ishladi.
words.example3: The doctor identified the problem early on, which made treatment much easier.
words.example3Translation: Shifokor muammoni dastlabki bosqichda aniqladi, bu davolashni ancha osonlashtirdi.
extraExamples: Early on, it was clear that the two candidates had very different visions.
They made some critical mistakes early on that were difficult to correct later.
Early on in the relationship, they established clear boundaries and expectations.
The film is slow early on, but it becomes very engaging later.
Early on in the game, one team scored twice and never looked back.
He showed a talent for music early on, taking his first lesson at age five.
Early on, scientists noticed that the experiment was producing unexpected results.
extraExampleTranslations: Dastlabki bosqichdayoq, ikki nomzodning juda xilma-xil tasavvurga ega ekanligi aniq edi.
Ular dastlabki bosqichda keyinchalik tuzatishi qiyin bo'lgan bir qancha muhim xatolar qildilar.
Munosabatlarning dastlabki bosqichida ular aniq chegaralar va kutilmalarni o'rnatdilar.
Film boshida sekin, lekin keyinchalik juda qiziqarli bo'lib qoladi.
O'yinning dastlabki bosqichida bir jamoa ikki marta gol urdi va hech orqaga qaramadi.
U besh yoshida birinchi darsini olib, musiqada iste'dod ko'rsatdi.
Dastlabki bosqichda olimlar tajriba kutilmagan natijalar berishi mumkinligini payqadilar.

words.definition: The amount of money an individual or household has left after paying taxes and essential living expenses such as rent, food, and bills.
words.definitionUz: Soliqlar va ijara, ovqat va kommunal to'lovlar kabi zaruriy yashash xarajatlarini to'lagandan keyin qoladigan pul miqdori.
words.example1: Rising rents have left many young people with very little disposable income.
words.example1Translation: Ko'tarilayotgan ijara narxlari ko'plab yosh odamlarni juda kam erkin sarflanadigan daromad bilan qoldirdi.
words.example2: Higher disposable income allows people to spend more on leisure activities.
words.example2Translation: Yuqori erkin sarflanadigan daromad odamlarga dam olish faoliyatlariga ko'proq sarflash imkonini beradi.
words.example3: The survey looked at how people choose to spend their disposable income.
words.example3Translation: So'rov odamlarning erkin sarflanadigan daromadlarini qanday sarflashni tanlashi bilan tanishdi.
extraExamples: During a recession, disposable income tends to fall as prices rise.
Tax cuts can increase disposable income for lower-earning households.
She used most of her disposable income to save for a house deposit.
The rise in energy bills reduced many families' disposable income significantly.
Businesses track disposable income trends to forecast consumer spending.
People with higher disposable income tend to travel more frequently.
A part-time job helped him increase his disposable income while studying.
extraExampleTranslations: Tanazzul davrida narxlar o'sishi bilan erkin sarflanadigan daromad kamayishga moyil.
Soliq imtiyozlari kam daromadli uy xo'jaliklari uchun erkin sarflanadigan daromadni oshirishi mumkin.
U erkin sarflanadigan daromadining aksariyatini uy uchun depozit to'plashga sarfladi.
Energiya hisob-fakturalarining o'sishi ko'plab oilalarning erkin sarflanadigan daromadini sezilarli darajada kamaytirdi.
Korxonalar iste'molchilarning xarajatlarini bashorat qilish uchun erkin sarflanadigan daromad tendensiyalarini kuzatib boradi.
Erkin sarflanadigan daromadi yuqori bo'lgan odamlar ko'proq sayohat qilishga moyil.
Qisman ish joyi o'qish davomida erkin sarflanadigan daromadini oshirishga yordam berdi.

words.translation: Diqqat bilan kuzatmoq, ko'zingizni ochiq tutmoq
words.definition: To stay watchful and alert, paying close attention in order to notice something when it appears.
words.definitionUz: Biror narsa paydo bo'lganda uni sezish uchun diqqatli va hushyor bo'lib, kuzatib turmoq.
words.example1: Keep your eyes peeled for a parking space — this area is usually very busy.
words.example1Translation: Avtoturargoh joyini qidiring — bu hudud odatda juda band bo'ladi.
words.example2: The detective told his partner to keep his eyes peeled for any suspicious activity.
words.example2Translation: Detektiv hamrohiga har qanday shubhali harakatni kuzatib turishni aytdi.
words.example3: Keep your eyes peeled — the celebrity was spotted near this area this morning.
words.example3Translation: Diqqat bilan kuzating — mashxur shaxs bu hudud yaqinida bugun ertalab ko'rilgan.
extraExamples: Keep your eyes peeled for the turning on the left — it is easy to miss.
While hiking, keep your eyes peeled for signs of wildlife.
We are looking for a good café, so keep your eyes peeled as we walk.
Keep your eyes peeled for any spelling errors before you submit the document.
The guide told tourists to keep their eyes peeled for dolphins in the water.
Keep your eyes peeled — the sale signs might appear in the window any day now.
He always keeps his eyes peeled for antique items when visiting flea markets.
extraExampleTranslations: Chapdagi burilishni kuzating — uni o'tkazib yuborish oson.
Piyoda yurish paytida yovvoyi tabiat belgilarini kuzatib boring.
Biz yaxshi kafe qidiryapmiz, shuning uchun yurish davomida diqqat bilan kuzating.
Hujjatni topshirishdan oldin imloviy xatolarni kuzating.
Yo'l-yo'riqchi turistlarga suvdagi delfinlarni kuzatib turishni aytdi.
Diqqat bilan kuzating — savdo belgilari har kuni derazada paydo bo'lishi mumkin.
U qo'l bozorlariga borganda har doim antik buyumlarni qidiradi.

words.definition: A naturally positive, optimistic, and happy temperament that a person consistently displays in their attitude and behaviour.
words.definitionUz: Odamning munosabati va xatti-harakatlarida izchil namoyon bo'ladigan tabiiy ijobiy, optimistik va baxtli xarakter.
words.example1: Her cheerful disposition made her popular with both students and teachers.
words.example1Translation: Uning quvnoq tabiati uni ham o'quvchilar, ham o'qituvchilar orasida mashhur qildi.
words.example2: Despite the difficult situation, he maintained his cheerful disposition throughout.
words.example2Translation: Qiyin vaziyatga qaramay, u butun vaqt davomida quvnoq tabiatini saqlab qoldi.
words.example3: A cheerful disposition can make a huge difference in a stressful work environment.
words.example3Translation: Quvnoq tabiat stressli ish muhitida katta farq yaratishi mumkin.
extraExamples: Her cheerful disposition lifted the mood of everyone in the team.
Even after setbacks, his cheerful disposition helped him recover quickly.
The nurse's cheerful disposition comforted patients during difficult treatments.
A cheerful disposition is one of the qualities employers most value in candidates.
He was known across the office for his cheerful disposition and constant smile.
Growing up in a loving family contributed to her cheerful disposition.
A cheerful disposition does not mean ignoring problems — it means facing them positively.
extraExampleTranslations: Uning quvnoq tabiati jamoadagi hamma kayfiyatini ko'tardi.
Muvaffaqiyatsizliklardan keyin ham uning quvnoq tabiati tezda o'ziga kelishiga yordam berdi.
Hamshiraning quvnoq tabiati bemorlarni qiyin davolash jarayonida tasalli berdi.
Quvnoq tabiat ish beruvchilar nomzodlarda eng ko'p qadrlaydigan sifatlardan biridir.
U butun ofisda quvnoq tabiati va doimiy tabassum bilan mashhur edi.
Mehr-muhabbatga to'la oilada o'sish uning quvnoq tabiatini shakllantirdi.
Quvnoq tabiat muammolarni e'tiborsiz qoldirish degani emas — bu ularga ijobiy yondashish degani.

words.definition: To gain the admiration and high regard of others through consistent behaviour, hard work, character, or achievements.
words.definitionUz: Izchil xatti-harakatlar, qattiq mehnat, xarakter yoki yutuqlar orqali boshqalarning hayratlantirishi va yuksak ehtiromini qozonmoq.
words.example1: She earned the respect of her colleagues by always keeping her promises.
words.example1Translation: U har doim va'dalarini bajarish orqali hamkasblarining hurmatini qozondi.
words.example2: The new coach earned the respect of his players through honesty and hard work.
words.example2Translation: Yangi murabbiy halollik va qattiq mehnat orqali o'yinchilarining hurmatini qozondi.
words.example3: It takes time and consistent effort to earn the respect of people around you.
words.example3Translation: Atrofingizda odamlarning hurmatini qozonish vaqt va izchil harakatni talab qiladi.
extraExamples: He earned the respect of the entire community through years of charitable work.
To earn the respect of your employees, you must treat them fairly and listen to them.
She earned the respect of her professors by consistently producing outstanding work.
Earning the respect of difficult colleagues is often more valuable than praise from easy ones.
His calm decision-making under pressure earned the respect of the whole team.
You cannot demand respect — you have to earn it through your actions.
She earned the respect of her opponents by competing with honesty and good sportsmanship.
extraExampleTranslations: U xayriya ishlari yillari davomida butun jamoaning hurmatini qozondi.
Xodimlariningizning hurmatini qozonish uchun ularga adolatli munosabatda bo'lish va tinglash kerak.
U professorlarining hurmatini izchil ravishda ajoyib ishlar yaratish orqali qozondi.
Qiyin hamkasblarning hurmatini qozonish ko'pincha oson hamkasblarning maqtovidan qimmatroq.
Bosim ostida xotirjam qarorlar qabul qilishi butun jamoaning hurmatini qozondi.
Hurmatni talab qilib bo'lmaydi — uni xatti-harakatlaring orqali qozonish kerak.
U halollik va sportsmenlik ruhi bilan musobaqaga kirishib, raqiblarining hurmatini qozondi.

words.definition: Used to confirm that something happened exactly as expected or predicted.
words.definitionUz: Biror narsa aynan kutilganidek yoki bashorat qilinganidek yuz berganini tasdiqlash uchun ishlatiladi.
words.example1: I thought it would rain, and sure enough, it started pouring an hour later.
words.example1Translation: Yomg'ir yog'ishini o'yladim va kutilganidek, bir soatdan keyin quyib yog'a boshladi.
words.example2: She said the film would be boring, and sure enough, half the audience left early.
words.example2Translation: U film zerikarli bo'lishini aytdi va kutilganidek, tomoshabinlarning yarmi erta chiqib ketdi.
words.example3: He warned me the traffic would be terrible, and sure enough, it took two hours to arrive.
words.example3Translation: U menga tiqilin juda yomon bo'lishini ogohlantirdi va kutilganidek, yetib kelish uchun ikki soat ketdi.
extraExamples: I suspected the report had errors, and sure enough, three mistakes were found.
They predicted the team would struggle, and sure enough, they lost the first five games.
She said the new café would be popular, and sure enough, there was a long queue outside.
He knew the interview would be tough, and sure enough, it lasted nearly three hours.
I left early to avoid the rush, and sure enough, the roads were packed by nine.
The weather forecast predicted snow, and sure enough, it arrived overnight.
Sure enough, just as everyone had hoped, the event was a huge success.
extraExampleTranslations: Hisobotda xatolar borligidan shubhalandim va kutilganidek, uchta xato topildi.
Ular jamoa qiynalishini bashorat qilishdi va kutilganidek, ular birinchi beshta o'yinni yutqazishdi.
U yangi kafe mashhur bo'lishini aytdi va kutilganidek, tashqarida uzun navbat bor edi.
U intervyu qiyin bo'lishini bilardi va kutilganidek, u deyarli uch soat davom etdi.
Shoshuvdan qochish uchun erta yo'lga chiqdim va kutilganidek, to'qqizga kelib yo'llar to'lib ketdi.
Ob-havo bashori qor yog'ishini bashorat qildi va kutilganidek, u tun bo'yi yog'di.
Kutilganidek, hamma umid qilganidek, tadbir katta muvaffaqiyat bo'ldi.

words.word: In the blink of an eye
words.definition: Extremely quickly; in a very short amount of time; almost instantaneously.
words.definitionUz: Juda tez; juda qisqa vaqt ichida; deyarli bir lahzada.
words.example1: The accident happened in the blink of an eye — no one had time to react.
words.example1Translation: Baxtsiz hodisa ko'z ochib yumguncha yuz berdi — hech kimning munosabat bildirish vaqti bo'lmadi.
words.example2: Technology can change our lives in the blink of an eye.
words.example2Translation: Texnologiya ko'z ochib yumguncha hayotimizni o'zgartirishi mumkin.
words.example3: She finished the test in the blink of an eye while others were still on the first page.
words.example3Translation: Boshqalar hali birinchi sahifada ekan, u ko'z ochib yumguncha testni tugatdi.
extraExamples: The opportunity disappeared in the blink of an eye, and he missed his chance.
In the blink of an eye, the child had run out of the front door.
Prices can change in the blink of an eye on busy financial markets.
The magician made the coin disappear in the blink of an eye.
Their lead in the match was lost in the blink of an eye after two quick goals.
The years go by in the blink of an eye when you are busy with work and family.
In the blink of an eye, the storm moved in and ruined the outdoor event.
extraExampleTranslations: Imkoniyat ko'z ochib yumguncha yo'qoldi va u fursatini o'tkazib yubordi.
Ko'z ochib yumguncha bola old eshikdan chiqib ketgan edi.
Narxlar gavjum moliyaviy bozorlarda ko'z ochib yumguncha o'zgarishi mumkin.
Sehrgar tangani ko'z ochib yumguncha yo'qoltirib yubordi.
O'yindagi ustunliklari ikki tezkor gol'dan keyin ko'z ochib yumguncha yo'qoldi.
Ish va oila bilan band bo'lganda yillar ko'z ochib yumguncha o'tib ketadi.
Ko'z ochib yumguncha bo'ron bostirib keldi va ochiq havoda o'tkaziladigan tadbirni buzdi.

words.definition: To be involved in an active disagreement, conflict, or argument with another person, group, or organization.
words.definitionUz: Boshqa kishi, guruh yoki tashkilot bilan faol kelishmovchilik, nizo yoki tortishuvda bo'lmoq.
words.example1: The company is currently in dispute with its suppliers over delivery delays.
words.example1Translation: Kompaniya hozirda yetkazib berish kechikishlari bo'yicha yetkazib beruvchilar bilan nizoda.
words.example2: He has been in dispute with his neighbour over the fence for over a year.
words.example2Translation: U bir yildan ortiq vaqt davomida to'siq masalasida qo'shnisi bilan nizoda bo'lib kelmoqda.
words.example3: The two nations remain in dispute with each other over the border region.
words.example3Translation: Ikki davlat chegara hududi bo'yicha bir-biri bilan nizoda bo'lib qolmoqda.
extraExamples: The union is in dispute with management over proposed pay cuts.
She found herself in dispute with the landlord after the repairs were not completed.
Both companies are in dispute with the regulator over the new licensing rules.
The athlete was in dispute with the club over the terms of his contract renewal.
Being in dispute with a business partner can be very stressful and costly.
The local council is in dispute with the developer over planning permissions.
They were in dispute with the airline for months before finally receiving compensation.
extraExampleTranslations: Kasaba uyushmasi taklif qilinayotgan ish haqi qisqartirishlari bo'yicha rahbariyat bilan nizoda.
Ta'mirlash ishlari bajarilmaganidan so'ng u uy egasi bilan nizoga tushib qoldi.
Ikki kompaniya ham yangi litsenziya qoidalari bo'yicha tartibga soluvchi bilan nizoda.
Sportchi shartnomani yangilash shartlari bo'yicha klub bilan nizoda edi.
Biznes hamkor bilan nizoda bo'lish juda stressli va qimmatga tushishi mumkin.
Mahalliy kengash qurilish ruxsatnomasi bo'yicha ishlab chiqaruvchi bilan nizoda.
Ular nihoyat kompensatsiya olishdan oldin bir necha oy davomida aviakompaniya bilan nizoda bo'lishdi.

words.definition: Unconventional, unusual, or slightly strange in one's behaviour or thinking, in a way that is often harmless but noticeably different from the norm.
words.definitionUz: O'z xatti-harakati yoki fikrlashida g'ayrioddiy, noodatiy yoki biroz g'alati bo'lish, bu ko'pincha zararsiz, lekin odatdan sezilarli darajada farq qiladigan tarzda.
words.example1: The eccentric professor wore mismatched shoes and spoke to his plants every morning.
words.example1Translation: G'alati professor mos kelmaydigan poyabzal kiyar va har ertalab o'simliklariga gapirardi.
words.example2: She was known as the eccentric artist who painted only at midnight.
words.example2Translation: U faqat yarim tunda rasm chizuvchi g'alati rassom sifatida tanilgan edi.
words.example3: His eccentric behaviour made him stand out but also made people curious about him.
words.example3Translation: Uning g'alati xatti-harakati uni ajralib turishiga sabab bo'ldi, lekin shu bilan birga odamlarni unga qiziqtirib qo'ydi.
extraExamples: The eccentric billionaire lived in a converted lighthouse on a remote island.
Her eccentric fashion sense involved combining colours that most people would never mix.
People found him eccentric but also genuinely creative and interesting.
The village had a reputation for its eccentric local characters.
Being slightly eccentric can be a great advantage in creative industries.
He was eccentric in his habits but completely reliable when it mattered most.
The children loved their eccentric grandmother and her unusual stories.
extraExampleTranslations: G'alati milliardchi masofadagi orolda qayta qurilgan mayoqda yashardi.
Uning g'alati moda hissi ko'pchilik hech qachon aralashtirmaydigan ranglarni birlashtirgan.
Odamlar uni g'alati, lekin haqiqatan ham ijodiy va qiziqarli deb topishdi.
Qishloqning g'alati mahalliy qahramonlari bo'lganligi uchun shuhrat qozongan edi.
Biroz g'alati bo'lish ijodiy sohalarda katta afzallik bo'lishi mumkin.
U odatlarida g'alati edi, lekin eng muhim paytlarda to'liq ishonchli edi.
Bolalar g'alati buvilarini va uning g'ayrioddiy hikoyalarini yaxshi ko'rishardi.

words.definition: To officially declare someone ineligible to participate in an activity, competition, or role, usually because they have broken a rule or failed to meet requirements.
words.definitionUz: Biror shaxsni odatda qoida buzgani yoki talablarga javob bermagani uchun faoliyat, musobaqa yoki rolda ishtirok etishga rasman yaroqsiz deb e'lon qilmoq.
words.example1: The athlete was disqualified from the race for using banned substances.
words.example1Translation: Sportchi taqiqlangan moddalardan foydalangani uchun poygadan diskvalifikatsiya qilindi.
words.example2: Arriving late to the audition automatically disqualified him from the competition.
words.example2Translation: Auditsiyaga kech kelishi uni musobaqadan avtomatik ravishda diskvalifikatsiya qildi.
words.example3: A criminal record can disqualify you from working in certain professions.
words.example3Translation: Jinoiy sud tizimdagi yozuv sizni ma'lum kasblar bilan shug'ullanishdan chetlatishi mumkin.
extraExamples: The team was disqualified from the tournament for fielding an ineligible player.
She was disqualified from the award ceremony for not meeting the entry criteria.
A positive drug test will disqualify any competitor immediately.
Falsifying documents can disqualify a candidate from the entire selection process.
The driver was disqualified from the race after ignoring a red flag signal.
Not declaring a conflict of interest can disqualify a judge from a case.
Failing the medical test disqualified him from joining the armed forces.
extraExampleTranslations: Jamoa musobaqadan muvofiq bo'lmagan o'yinchini o'yinga tushirgani uchun diskvalifikatsiya qilindi.
U kirishga mezonlarga javob bermagani uchun mukofot marosimidan diskvalifikatsiya qilindi.
Ijobiy doping testi har qanday ishtirokchini darhol diskvalifikatsiya qiladi.
Hujjatlarni soxtalashtirish nomzodning butun tanlov jarayonidan diskvalifikatsiya qilinishiga olib kelishi mumkin.
Haydovchi qizil bayroq signalini e'tiborsiz qoldirgani uchun poygadan diskvalifikatsiya qilindi.
Manfaatlar to'qnashuvini e'lon qilmaslik sudyaning ishdan chetlatilishiga olib kelishi mumkin.
Tibbiy testdan o'tolmaganlik uni qurolli kuchlarga qo'shilishdan chetladi.

words.translation: Pastga qarab aylanaga kirmoq, tobora yomonlashuv yo'liga o'tmoq
words.definition: To enter a progressively worsening situation in which each problem makes the next one worse, leading to a continuous and accelerating decline.
words.definitionUz: Har bir muammo keyingisini yanada og'irlashtirgan holda, uzluksiz va tezlashuvchan pasayishga olib keladigan tobora yomonlashuvchi vaziyatga kirmoq.
words.example1: After losing his job, he went into a downward spiral of debt and depression.
words.example1Translation: Ishini yo'qotganidan so'ng, u qarz va depressiya halqasiga tushib qoldi.
words.example2: The company went into a downward spiral after it lost its biggest client.
words.example2Translation: Kompaniya eng yirik mijozini yo'qotgandan keyin tobora yomonlashuv yo'liga o'tdi.
words.example3: Without support, young people struggling with addiction can go into a downward spiral.
words.example3Translation: Qo'llab-quvvatlashsiz giyohvandlik bilan kurashayotgan yosh odamlar tobora yomonlashuv halqasiga tushishi mumkin.
extraExamples: The athlete went into a downward spiral after a series of injuries disrupted her season.
Poor financial decisions sent the small business into a downward spiral.
He noticed his mental health going into a downward spiral and sought help immediately.
Once the team's confidence broke, their results went into a downward spiral.
Recognizing early signs can help prevent a person from going into a downward spiral.
The city went into a downward spiral after its main industry collapsed.
Their relationship went into a downward spiral after trust was completely broken.
extraExampleTranslations: Sportchi bir qator shikastlanishlar mavsumini buzgandan so'ng tobora yomonlashuv yo'liga o'tdi.
Noto'g'ri moliyaviy qarorlar kichik biznesni tobora yomonlashuv halqasiga kiritdi.
U ruhiy sog'lig'ining tobora yomonlashayotganini payqadi va darhol yordam so'radi.
Jamoaning ishonchi singanidan so'ng, natijalari tobora yomonlashib ketdi.
Erta belgilarni tanib olish odamni tobora yomonlashuv halqasiga kirishining oldini olishga yordam beradi.
Shahardagi asosiy sanoat qulaganidan so'ng shahar tobora yomonlashuv yo'liga o'tdi.
Ularning munosabatlari ishonch butunlay singanidan so'ng tobora yomonlashuv halqasiga kirdi.

words.definition: In one's imagination or mental vision; the ability to visualize or picture something in your mind without actually seeing it.
words.definitionUz: Xayolda yoki aqliy tasavvurda; aslida ko'rmasdan biror narsani aqliy ko'rish yoki tasavvur qilish qobiliyati.
words.example1: In my mind's eye, I could clearly picture the old house I grew up in.
words.example1Translation: Tasavvurimda men katta bo'lgan eski uyni aniq ko'ra olardim.
words.example2: The writer described the scene so vividly that it appeared in my mind's eye.
words.example2Translation: Yozuvchi sahnani shunchalik jonli tasvirladiki, u tasavvurimda ko'rindi.
words.example3: In your mind's eye, try to visualize the outcome you want to achieve.
words.example3Translation: Tasavvuringizda erishmoqchi bo'lgan natijani ko'rishga harakat qiling.
extraExamples: In his mind's eye, he could see the finished painting before he even picked up the brush.
She revisited her childhood home in her mind's eye during a moment of nostalgia.
The architect held the entire design in her mind's eye throughout the project.
In your mind's eye, picture a calm beach and let your stress slowly fade away.
Great storytellers make characters come alive in the reader's mind's eye.
In his mind's eye, he had already won the race before the starting gun was fired.
The instructor asked the students to hold the finished product in their mind's eye.
extraExampleTranslations: U mo'yqalamni olishdan oldin ham tasavvurida tugallangan rasmni ko'ra olardi.
U nostalji daqiqasida xayolan bolaligi o'tgan uyiga qaytdi.
Arxitektor butun loyiha davomida butun dizaynni tasavvurida saqladi.
Tasavvuringizda tinch pludni ko'ring va stressingiz asta-sekin yo'q bo'lishiga yo'l qo'ying.
Zo'r hikoyachilar qahramonlarni o'quvchi tasavvurida jonlantirishadi.
U xayolan irmoq o'qqa tutilishidan oldin poygani allaqachon yutib bo'lgan edi.
Muallim o'quvchilardan tayyor mahsulotni tasavvurlarida saqlashlarini so'radi.

words.definition: Actions or conduct that disturb, interrupt, or prevent the normal functioning of an environment such as a classroom, workplace, or public setting.
words.definitionUz: Sinf, ish joyi yoki jamoat joyidagi normal ishlashni buzadigan, to'xtatadigan yoki oldini oladigan harakatlar yoki xulq-atvor.
words.example1: The teacher asked the student to leave after repeated disruptive behaviour in class.
words.example1Translation: O'qituvchi sinfda qayta-qayta tartibni buzuvchi xatti-harakatdan so'ng o'quvchidan chiqib ketishini so'radi.
words.example2: Disruptive behaviour in the workplace can affect the productivity of the whole team.
words.example2Translation: Ish joyidagi tartibni buzuvchi xatti-harakat butun jamoa samaradorligiga ta'sir qilishi mumkin.
words.example3: The school has a clear policy for dealing with disruptive behaviour.
words.example3Translation: Maktabda tartibni buzuvchi xatti-harakatni hal qilish uchun aniq siyosat mavjud.
extraExamples: Disruptive behaviour during a flight can result in the passenger being removed from the aircraft.
Parents were called in to discuss their child's disruptive behaviour.
Some students display disruptive behaviour because they are struggling academically.
The manager warned the employee that further disruptive behaviour would not be tolerated.
Disruptive behaviour in public spaces can make others feel unsafe.
Early intervention is the most effective way to address disruptive behaviour in young children.
The conference was delayed due to disruptive behaviour from a small group of protesters.
extraExampleTranslations: Parvoz paytida tartibni buzuvchi xatti-harakat yo'lovchining samolyotdan chiqarib yuborilishiga olib kelishi mumkin.
Ota-onalar farzandlarining tartibni buzuvchi xatti-harakatini muhokama qilish uchun chaqirildi.
Ba'zi o'quvchilar akademik jihatdan qiynalayotganliklari uchun tartibni buzuvchi xatti-harakat ko'rsatadi.
Menejer xodimni bundan keyingi tartibni buzuvchi xatti-harakatga toqat qilinmasligini ogohlantirdi.
Jamoat joylarida tartibni buzuvchi xatti-harakat boshqalarni o'zlarini xavfsiz his qilmasligiga olib kelishi mumkin.
Erta aralashuv yosh bolalarda tartibni buzuvchi xatti-harakatni hal qilishning eng samarali usuli.
Anjuman kichik bir norozilarkchilar guruhining tartibni buzuvchi xatti-harakati tufayli kechikdi.

words.translation: Ustunlikka ega bo'lmoq, biroz afzalroq bo'lmoq
words.definition: To have a slight but meaningful advantage over a competitor, rival, or alternative in a particular context.
words.definitionUz: Ma'lum bir kontekstda raqib, qarama-qarshi tomon yoki muqobilga nisbatan kichik lekin muhim ustunlikka ega bo'lmoq.
words.example1: Our product has the edge over competitors because of its superior battery life.
words.example1Translation: Bizning mahsulotimiz ustun batareya muddati tufayli raqiblardan ustunlikka ega.
words.example2: Years of experience gave her the edge over younger candidates in the interview.
words.example2Translation: Ko'p yillik tajriba unga intervyuda yoshroq nomzodlar ustidan ustunlik berdi.
words.example3: The home team usually has the edge over visiting teams due to crowd support.
words.example3Translation: Uy jamoasi odatda tomoshabinlar qo'llab-quvvatlashi tufayli mehmon jamoalardan ustunlikka ega.
extraExamples: Speaking multiple languages gives candidates the edge over monolingual applicants.
Their faster delivery service gives them the edge over other online retailers.
He had the edge over his rivals because of his exceptional technical knowledge.
Good customer service can give a small business the edge over larger competitors.
The team had the edge over others due to months of intensive preparation.
Having a personal connection to the client gave her the edge over other bidders.
In a tight race, even small details can give one candidate the edge over another.
extraExampleTranslations: Bir necha tilda gapira bilish nomzodlarga bir tilli ariza beruvchilardan ustunlik beradi.
Ularning tezroq yetkazib berish xizmati ularga boshqa onlayn chakana savdochilar ustidan ustunlik beradi.
Uning g'ayritabiiy texnik bilimlari unga raqiblaridan ustunlik berdi.
Yaxshi mijozlarga xizmat ko'rsatish kichik biznesga yirik raqiblardan ustunlik berishi mumkin.
Jamoa oylik intensiv tayyorgarlik tufayli boshqalardan ustunlikka ega edi.
Mijoz bilan shaxsiy aloqa unga boshqa taklif beruvchilardan ustunlik berdi.
Qattiq poygada hatto kichik tafsilotlar ham bir nomzodga boshqasidan ustunlik berishi mumkin.

words.definition: The fundamental structure, values, relationships, and institutions that hold a society together and give it its character.
words.definitionUz: Jamiyatni birlashtiruvchi va unga xarakter beradigan asosiy tuzilma, qadriyatlar, munosabatlar va muassasalar.
words.example1: Corruption threatens to tear apart the very fabric of society.
words.example1Translation: Korrupsiya jamiyatning asosini yo'q qilish bilan tahdid qilmoqda.
words.example2: Shared values and mutual respect are essential threads in the fabric of society.
words.example2Translation: Umumiy qadriyatlar va o'zaro hurmat jamiyatning asosidagi muhim iplardir.
words.example3: High unemployment can seriously damage the fabric of society over time.
words.example3Translation: Yuqori ishsizlik vaqt o'tishi bilan jamiyatning asosiga jiddiy zarar yetkazishi mumkin.
extraExamples: Trust between citizens and institutions is central to the fabric of society.
War can destroy the fabric of society in ways that take generations to repair.
Strong families and communities form the backbone of the fabric of society.
Education is one of the most important threads in the fabric of society.
When inequality grows too great, it weakens the fabric of society.
Volunteering and civic engagement strengthen the fabric of society.
The rapid pace of change is challenging the traditional fabric of society.
extraExampleTranslations: Fuqarolar va muassasalar o'rtasidagi ishonch jamiyat asosining markazida.
Urush jamiyat asosini avlodlar davomida tuzatish talab qiladigan tarzda yo'q qilishi mumkin.
Kuchli oilalar va jamoatchilik jamiyat asosining umurtqa pog'onasini tashkil etadi.
Ta'lim jamiyat asosidagi eng muhim iplardan biridir.
Tengsizlik juda kattayib ketganda, u jamiyat asosini zaiflаshtiradi.
Ko'ngillilik va fuqarolik faoliyati jamiyat asosini mustahkamlaydi.
O'zgarishning tez sur'ati jamiyatning an'anaviy asosini sinovdan o'tkazmoqda.

words.definition: A family member who is not closely related, such as a second or third cousin, or someone several generations removed.
words.definitionUz: Ikkinchi yoki uchinchi darajali amaki/xola farzandlari yoki bir necha avlod oldin ajralganlar kabi yaqin emas, balki uzoq qarindosh.
words.example1: A distant relative I had never met left me a small inheritance.
words.example1Translation: Men hech qachon ko'rmagan uzoq qarindoshim menga kichik meros qoldirdi.
words.example2: At the family reunion, we met distant relatives from three different countries.
words.example2Translation: Oilaviy uchrashuvda biz uchta turli mamlakatdan uzoq qarindoshlar bilan uchrасhdik.
words.example3: He discovered through a DNA test that the famous artist was a distant relative.
words.example3Translation: U DNK testi orqali mashhur rassom uzoq qarindoshi ekanligini aniqladi.
extraExamples: She received a letter from a distant relative she had not heard from in thirty years.
A distant relative tracked down the family through an online genealogy website.
Distant relatives often share fewer physical traits than immediate family members.
He was surprised to learn that a distant relative had founded the town centuries ago.
Even distant relatives can offer unexpected support in times of need.
Through social media, she reconnected with a distant relative living on another continent.
The estate was passed on to a distant relative after no close family could be found.
extraExampleTranslations: U o'ttiz yil davomida xabar olmaydigan uzoq qarindoshidan maktub oldi.
Uzoq qarindosh oilaviy genealogiya veb-sayti orqali oilani topdi.
Uzoq qarindoshlar ko'pincha yaqin oila a'zolari bilan solishtirganda kamroq jismoniy xususiyatlarni baham ko'rishadi.
U uzoq qarindoshi asrlar oldin bu qishloqqa asos solganini bilganda hayron bo'ldi.
Hatto uzoq qarindoshlar ham ehtiyoj vaqtida kutilmagan qo'llab-quvvatlash taklif qilishi mumkin.
Ijtimoiy tarmoqlar orqali u boshqa qit'ada yashaydigan uzoq qarindoshi bilan qayta bog'landi.
Yaqin oila topilmaganidan so'ng mulk uzoq qarindoshga o'tkazildi.

words.definition: Highly respected, distinguished, and well-known within a particular field or area of expertise.
words.definitionUz: Ma'lum bir soha yoki mutaxassislik yo'nalishida yuqori darajada hurmat qozongan, taniqli va mashhur.
words.example1: An eminent scientist was invited to speak at the international conference.
words.example1Translation: Xalqaro konferensiyada nutq so'zlash uchun ko'zga ko'ringan olim taklif qilindi.
words.example2: She is an eminent historian whose work has influenced the entire academic field.
words.example2Translation: U butun akademik sohaga ta'sir ko'rsatgan ko'zga ko'ringan tarixchi.
words.example3: The award was presented by one of the most eminent figures in the legal profession.
words.example3Translation: Mukofotni huquqshunoslik kasbidagi eng ko'zga ko'ringan shaxslardan biri topshirdi.
extraExamples: The eminent philosopher spent decades challenging mainstream academic thinking.
Her work was reviewed by a panel of eminent experts from around the world.
The hospital recruited an eminent surgeon to lead its new cardiac unit.
Eminent scholars from several universities contributed to the research report.
He became one of the most eminent figures in modern architecture by the age of forty.
An eminent judge was appointed to oversee the independent inquiry.
The eminent writer was celebrated not only for her prose but also her moral courage.
extraExampleTranslations: Ko'zga ko'ringan faylasuf o'n yillar davomida asosiy akademik fikrga qarshi chiqdi.
Uning ishi butun dunyo bo'ylab ko'zga ko'ringan ekspertlar kengashi tomonidan ko'rib chiqildi.
Kasalxona yangi yurak bo'limiga rahbarlik qilish uchun ko'zga ko'ringan jarrohni jalb qildi.
Bir nechta universitetlardan ko'zga ko'ringan olimlar tadqiqot hisobotiga hissa qo'shdilar.
U qirq yoshiga kelib zamonaviy arxitekturadagi eng ko'zga ko'ringan shaxslardan biriga aylandi.
Mustaqil so'rov o'tkazish uchun ko'zga ko'ringan sudya tayinlandi.
Ko'zga ko'ringan yozuvchi nafaqat nasri, balki axloqiy jasorati uchun ham ulug'landi.

words.definition: To continue for an excessively or unnecessarily long period of time, often becoming tedious or frustrating.
words.definitionUz: Ko'pincha zerikarli yoki asabiylashtirib, haddan ziyod yoki keraksiz uzoq davom etmoq.
words.example1: The court case dragged on for months before a verdict was finally reached.
words.example1Translation: Sudlov jarayoni nihoyat hukm chiqarilgunga qadar oylarca cho'zildi.
words.example2: What was supposed to be a quick meeting dragged on for months of negotiations.
words.example2Translation: Tez bo'lib o'tishi kerak bo'lgan uchrashuv oylarca muzokaralarga cho'zildi.
words.example3: The construction work dragged on for months, causing constant disruption to local residents.
words.example3Translation: Qurilish ishlari oylarca cho'zilib, mahalliy aholini doimiy ravishda bezovta qildi.
extraExamples: The negotiations dragged on for months without any sign of progress.
Her recovery from surgery dragged on for months longer than the doctors had expected.
The investigation dragged on for months, keeping everyone in a state of uncertainty.
The dispute between the two companies dragged on for months before being settled out of court.
Fans grew frustrated as the transfer saga dragged on for months without a resolution.
The project dragged on for months because of poor planning and lack of resources.
The rainy season dragged on for months, causing widespread flooding across the region.
extraExampleTranslations: Muzokaralar hech qanday rivojlanish ko'rinmay oylarca cho'zildi.
Uning jarrohlikdan keyin tiklanishi shifokorlar kutganidan oylarca ko'proq cho'zildi.
Tergov oylarca cho'zilib, hamma noaniqlik holatida qoldi.
Ikki kompaniya o'rtasidagi nizo suddan tashqarida hal qilinishidan oldin oylarca cho'zildi.
Muxlislar o'tkazish dramasi hal qilinmay oylarca cho'zilgani uchun asabiy bo'ldi.
Loyiha yomon rejalashtirish va resurslar etishmasligi sababli oylarca cho'zildi.
Yomg'irli mavsum oylarca cho'zilib, mintaqada keng miqyosli suv bosishiga olib keldi.

words.definition: To experience a loss of respect, reputation, or dignity in the eyes of others, often as a result of a public failure or embarrassment.
words.definitionUz: Ko'pincha ommaviy muvaffaqiyatsizlik yoki uyat natijasida boshqalar ko'zida hurmat, obro' yoki qadr-qimmatini yo'qotmoq.
words.example1: The manager refused to admit the mistake because he didn't want to lose face in front of the team.
words.example1Translation: Menejer jamoa oldida obro'sini yo'qotmaslik uchun xatoni tan olishdan bosh tortdi.
words.example2: Apologizing publicly made him feel like he would lose face, even though it was the right thing to do.
words.example2Translation: U ommaviy ravishda kechirim so'rash to'g'ri ish bo'lsa-da, obro'sini yo'qotadigandek his qildirdi.
words.example3: In many Asian cultures, causing someone to lose face is considered deeply disrespectful.
words.example3Translation: Ko'plab Osiyo madaniyatlarida birovning obro'sini yo'qotishiga sabab bo'lish juda hurmatsizlik deb hisoblanadi.
extraExamples: The politician feared that backing down from his position would cause him to lose face with voters.
She would rather struggle alone than ask for help and lose face.
The company chose not to issue a recall publicly, worried about losing face with investors.
He lost face when his overconfident prediction turned out to be completely wrong.
Negotiators worked carefully to find a compromise that would allow both sides to avoid losing face.
Admitting you don't know something in a meeting doesn't have to mean losing face.
In competitive environments, people sometimes make bad decisions just to avoid losing face.
extraExampleTranslations: Siyosatchi o'z pozitsiyasidan chekinish uni saylovchilar oldida obro'sini yo'qotishiga olib kelishidan qo'rqdi.
U yordam so'rab obro'sini yo'qotishdan ko'ra yolg'iz kurashishni afzal ko'radi.
Kompaniya investorlar oldida obro'sini yo'qotishdan qo'rqib, qaytarib olishni ommaviy e'lon qilmaslikni tanladi.
U haddan oshiq ishonch bilan qilgan bashorati butunlay noto'g'ri bo'lib chiqqanida obro'sini yo'qotdi.
Muzokarachilar har ikkala tomonga obro'sini yo'qotmaslikka imkon beradigan murosani topish uchun ehtiyotkorlik bilan ishladi.
Uchrashuvda biror narsani bilmasligingizni tan olish obro'ingizni yo'qotish demak emas.
Raqobatbardosh muhitlarda odamlar ba'zan faqat obro'sini yo'qotmaslik uchun noto'g'ri qarorlar qabul qilishadi.

words.definition: Visual content — such as photographs or video footage — that causes feelings of shock, distress, disgust, or psychological discomfort in the viewer.
words.definitionUz: Tomosha qiluvchida hayrat, bezovtalik, jirkanch yoki psixologik noqulaylik hissini uyg'otuvchi fotosuratlar yoki video lavhalar kabi vizual kontent.
words.example1: The documentary contained disturbing images of the aftermath of the natural disaster.
words.example1Translation: Hujjatli film tabiiy ofat oqibatlarining bezovta qiluvchi tasvirlarini o'z ichiga oldi.
words.example2: Viewer discretion was advised as the news report included disturbing images from the conflict zone.
words.example2Translation: Yangiliklar xabari mojarolar zonasidagi bezovta qiluvchi tasvirlarni o'z ichiga olgani uchun tomoshabinlarga ehtiyotkorlik tavsiya etildi.
words.example3: Social media platforms have struggled to remove disturbing images quickly enough.
words.example3Translation: Ijtimoiy media platformalari bezovta qiluvchi tasvirlarni yetarlicha tez olib tashlashda qiynaldi.
extraExamples: The campaign used disturbing images to raise awareness about animal cruelty.
Children should be protected from exposure to disturbing images online.
The reporter warned audiences before showing the disturbing images captured at the scene.
Many people find it difficult to forget disturbing images even long after seeing them.
News editors face difficult decisions about whether to publish disturbing images from war zones.
The exhibition featured disturbing images that challenged visitors to confront uncomfortable realities.
Content warnings are placed before disturbing images to give viewers the choice to look away.
extraExampleTranslations: Kampaniya hayvonlarga shafqatsizlik haqida xabardorlikni oshirish uchun bezovta qiluvchi tasvirlardan foydalandi.
Bolalar onlaynda bezovta qiluvchi tasvirlarga duch kelishdan himoya qilinishi kerak.
Muxbir joyda suratga olingan bezovta qiluvchi tasvirlarni ko'rsatishdan oldin tomoshabinlarni ogohlantirdi.
Ko'p odamlar bezovta qiluvchi tasvirlarni ko'rganidan ancha vaqt o'tgandan keyin ham unutish qiyin deb topishadi.
Yangilik muharrirlari urush zonalaridan bezovta qiluvchi tasvirlarni nashr etish kerakmi degan qiyin qarorga duch keladi.
Ko'rgazma tashrif buyuruvchilarni noqulay haqiqatlar bilan yuzma-yuz qiluvchi bezovta qiluvchi tasvirlarni o'z ichiga oldi.
Kontent ogohlantirishlari tomoshabinlarga noshaf nazar solmaslik imkoniyatini berish uchun bezovta qiluvchi tasvirlar oldiga qo'yiladi.

words.definition: To release or discharge gases into the surrounding atmosphere, often as a byproduct of industrial, natural, or biological processes.
words.definitionUz: Ko'pincha sanoat, tabiiy yoki biologik jarayonlarning yon mahsuli sifatida atrof-muhitga gazlar chiqarish yoki ajratish.
words.example1: Factories that emit gases without proper filters are contributing to serious air pollution.
words.example1Translation: Tegishli filtrlar o'rnatmasdan gaz chiqaradigan zavodlar jiddiy havo ifloslanishiga hissa qo'shmoqda.
words.example2: Volcanic eruptions can emit gases that affect air quality thousands of kilometers away.
words.example2Translation: Vulqon otilishlari minglab kilometr uzoqlikdagi havo sifatiga ta'sir qiladigan gazlar chiqarishi mumkin.
words.example3: Cars that emit gases above legal limits can be fined and taken off the road.
words.example3Translation: Qonuniy chegaradan ortiq gaz chiqaradigan avtomobillar jarimaga tortilishi va yo'ldan olib tashlanishi mumkin.
extraExamples: Landfill sites emit gases such as methane as organic waste decomposes underground.
Livestock farming emit gases that contribute significantly to global greenhouse gas levels.
Scientists measure how much carbon dioxide power plants emit gases into the atmosphere each year.
Some bacteria emit gases as part of their natural digestive processes.
New regulations require industries to reduce the amount of harmful gases they emit.
Electric vehicles were developed partly to replace combustion engines that emit gases into cities.
Deforestation causes trees to release stored carbon, which effectively means the land starts to emit gases.
extraExampleTranslations: Poligon maydonlari organik chiqindilar yer ostida parchalanishi bilan metan kabi gazlar chiqaradi.
Chorvachilik global issiqxona gazlari darajasiga sezilarli hissa qo'shadigan gazlar chiqaradi.
Olimlar elektr stantsiyalari har yili atmosferaga qancha karbonat angidrid chiqarishini o'lchaydi.
Ba'zi bakteriyalar tabiiy hazm qilish jarayonlarining bir qismi sifatida gazlar chiqaradi.
Yangi qoidalar sanoatdan chiqaradigan zararli gazlar miqdorini kamaytirishni talab qiladi.
Elektr avtomobillari qisman shaharlarda gaz chiqaradigan yonuv dvigatellarini almashtirish uchun ishlab chiqildi.
O'rmonlarni kesish daraxtlarning saqlangan uglerodini chiqarishiga sabab bo'ladi, bu aslida yer gaz chiqara boshlaganini anglatadi.

words.definition: To start doing something with great enthusiasm, energy, and commitment, often without hesitation.
words.definitionUz: Ko'pincha ikkilanmasdan katta ishtiyoq, energiya va sadoqat bilan biror narsani boshlash.
words.example1: She dove into the new project with so much energy that she worked through the weekend.
words.example1Translation: U yangi loyihaga shunchalik energiya bilan kirishib ketdiki, dam olish kunlari ham ishladi.
words.example2: Instead of overthinking it, just dive into the task and figure things out as you go.
words.example2Translation: Haddan ziyod o'ylamay, shunchaki vazifaga bosh qo'shib kiring va yo'l-yo'lakay tushunib olasiz.
words.example3: He loves to dive into a good book at the end of a long day.
words.example3Translation: U uzoq kunning oxirida yaxshi kitobga bosh qo'shishni yaxshi ko'radi.
extraExamples: The students were eager to dive into the experiment as soon as the teacher explained it.
After years of hesitation, she finally decided to dive into learning a new language.
Don't wait for perfect conditions — sometimes you just have to dive into a challenge.
He dove into the research so deeply that he lost track of time.
The new employee dove into their responsibilities from day one, impressing everyone.
Let's dive into the details of the plan before we make any final decisions.
She dove into cooking as a hobby during the lockdown and discovered a real passion for it.
extraExampleTranslations: O'quvchilar o'qituvchi tushuntirgandan so'ng darhol eksperimentga bosh qo'shishga sabrsiz edilar.
Yillar davomida ikkilangandan so'ng, u nihoyat yangi til o'rganishga bosh qo'shishga qaror qildi.
Mukammal sharoitlarni kutmang — ba'zan shunchaki muammoga bosh qo'shib kirish kerak.
U tadqiqotga shunchalik chuqur bosh qo'shdiki, vaqtni unutib qo'ydi.
Yangi xodim birinchi kundan boshlab mas'uliyatlariga bosh qo'shib, hammani hayratda qoldirdi.
Hech qanday yakuniy qaror qabul qilishdan oldin rejaning tafsilotlariga bosh qo'shaylik.
U karantin paytida oshpazlikka berilib ketdi va bunga chinakam ishtiyoq topdi.

words.definition: Deoxyribonucleic acid — the molecule found in the cells of all living organisms that carries the genetic information determining their development, functioning, and inherited traits.
words.definitionUz: Dezoksiribonuklein kislota — barcha tirik organizmlarning hujayralarida mavjud bo'lib, ularning rivojlanishi, faoliyati va meros xususiyatlarini belgilovchi genetik ma'lumotlarni tashuvchi molekula.
words.example1: DNA evidence from the crime scene helped identify the suspect.
words.example1Translation: Jinoyat joyidagi DNK dalillari gumonlanuvchini aniqlashga yordam berdi.
words.example2: Scientists use DNA analysis to trace evolutionary relationships between different species.
words.example2Translation: Olimlar turli turning evolyutsion munosabatlarini kuzatish uchun DNK tahlilidan foydalanadi.
words.example3: Each person's DNA is unique, which is why it can be used to identify individuals.
words.example3Translation: Har bir odamning DNKsi noyob, shu sababli u shaxslarni aniqlash uchun ishlatilishi mumkin.
extraExamples: A DNA test revealed that the two strangers were actually distant cousins.
Researchers are studying how certain DNA mutations can lead to inherited diseases.
DNA can survive for thousands of years in the right conditions, helping scientists study ancient humans.
The double helix structure of DNA was first described by Watson and Crick in 1953.
Gene therapy involves altering a patient's DNA to treat or prevent disease.
DNA profiling has become a powerful tool in solving cold cases decades after they occurred.
Some athletes have had their DNA analyzed to identify their natural strengths and weaknesses.
extraExampleTranslations: DNK testi ikki begonaning aslida uzoq qarindosh ekanligini aniqladi.
Tadqiqotchilar ma'lum DNK mutatsiyalari qanday qilib irsiy kasalliklarga olib kelishini o'rganmoqda.
DNK to'g'ri sharoitlarda minglab yillar davomida saqlanib qolishi mumkin va bu olimlar qadimgi odamlarni o'rganishga yordam beradi.
DNKning ikki spiralli tuzilishi birinchi marta 1953-yilda Uotson va Krik tomonidan tasvirlangan.
Gen terapiyasi kasallikni davolash yoki oldini olish uchun bemorning DNKsini o'zgartirishni o'z ichiga oladi.
DNK profillashtirish sodir bo'lganidan o'n yillar o'tib sovuq ishlarni hal qilishda kuchli vositaga aylandi.
Ba'zi sportchilar tabiiy kuchli va zaif tomonlarini aniqlash uchun DNKlarini tahlil qildirdi.

words.definition: To manage to maintain a serious, neutral facial expression without laughing or smiling, especially in a situation that is funny or amusing.
words.definitionUz: Ayniqsa kulgili yoki qiziqarli vaziyatda kulmasdan yoki jilmaymasdan jiddiy, neytral yuz ifodasini saqlash.
words.example1: The comedian was so funny that the interviewer could barely keep a straight face.
words.example1Translation: Komediyachi shunchalik kulgili ediki, suhbatdosh zo'rg'a jiddiy qiyofasini saqladi.
words.example2: It's very hard to keep a straight face when someone tells a terrible joke very seriously.
words.example2Translation: Kimdir yomon hazilni juda jiddiy aytganda jiddiy qiyofani saqlash juda qiyin.
words.example3: She managed to keep a straight face while delivering the prank to her colleague.
words.example3Translation: U hamkasbi bilan hazil qilayotganda jiddiy qiyofasini saqlashga muvaffaq bo'ldi.
extraExamples: The actors struggled to keep a straight face during the blooper reel you can see at the end of the film.
How do you keep a straight face when your child says something unintentionally hilarious?
The judge had to keep a straight face as the defendant gave an increasingly absurd explanation.
I could not keep a straight face when my friend walked into the surprise party.
Good poker players train themselves to keep a straight face no matter what cards they hold.
The teacher tried to keep a straight face while scolding the students for their prank.
Keeping a straight face in a formal meeting when something goes wrong takes real self-control.
extraExampleTranslations: Aktvorlar filmning oxirida ko'rishingiz mumkin bo'lgan xatolar lavhasida jiddiy qiyofalarini saqlashda qiynalishdi.
Farzandingiz tasodifan kulgili narsa aytganda qanday qilib jiddiy qiyofangizni saqlay olasiz?
Sudya da'vogar tobora bema'ni tushuntirish berar ekan, jiddiy qiyofasini saqlashi kerak edi.
Do'stim kutilmagan ziyofatga kirib kelganida jiddiy qiyofamni saqlayolmadim.
Yaxshi poker o'yinchilari qanday kartalar ushlab turganlaridan qat'iy nazar jiddiy qiyofalarini saqlashga o'rganishadi.
O'qituvchi o'quvchilarning hazili uchun ularni tanbehlayotganda jiddiy qiyofasini saqlashga harakat qildi.
Rasmiy uchrashuvda biror narsa noto'g'ri ketganda jiddiy qiyofani saqlash haqiqiy o'z-o'zini nazorat talab etadi.

words.definition: To cause extreme physical or emotional exhaustion, leaving a person feeling completely depleted of energy or strength.
words.definitionUz: Odamni energiya yoki kuchdan butunlay quriganday his qildiriб, juda kuchli jismoniy yoki hissiy holdan tolishni keltirib chiqarmoq.
words.example1: Taking care of my sick parent for months completely drained me.
words.example1Translation: Oy davomida kasal ota-onamga qarash meni butunlay holdan toldirdi.
words.example2: The long-haul flight and the time zone changes absolutely drained me.
words.example2Translation: Uzoq parvoz va vaqt mintaqasi o'zgarishlari meni batamom holdan toldirdi.
words.example3: That argument with my boss really drained me emotionally — I couldn't focus for the rest of the day.
words.example3Translation: Boshlig'im bilan o'sha bahs meni hissiy jihatdan shunchalik holdan toldirilki, kun bo'yi diqqatimni jamlay olmadim.
extraExamples: Dealing with constant negativity from others really drained me over time.
The weeks of intense revision before the exams totally drained me.
She said that working two jobs simultaneously had completely drained her.
It drained me to keep pretending everything was fine when it wasn't.
Even enjoyable social events can drain me if they go on too long.
The emotional labor of supporting everyone around me finally drained me.
Running the marathon was an incredible achievement, but it absolutely drained me for days afterward.
extraExampleTranslations: Boshqalarning doimiy salbiyligiga dosh berish vaqt o'tishi bilan meni holdan toldirdi.
Imtihon oldidan haftalar davom etgan kuchli tayyorgarlik meni tamoman holdan toldirdi.
U bir vaqtda ikki ish qilish uni butunlay holdan toldirgani aytdi.
Hamma yaxshi emas ekan-da, hammasi yaxshiligini ko'rsatishni davom ettirish meni holdan toldirdi.
Qiziqarli ijtimoiy tadbirlar ham juda cho'zilib ketsa meni holdan toldirishi mumkin.
Atrofimda hammani qo'llab-quvvatlashning hissiy mehnati nihoyat meni holdan toldirdi.
Marafon yugurish ajoyib yutuq edi, lekin bir necha kun davomida meni butunlay holdan toldirdi.

words.definition: Far exceeding anything that one could have imagined, hoped for, or expected, in a remarkably positive and surprising way.
words.definitionUz: Odamning tasavvur qilishi, umid qilishi yoki kutishi mumkin bo'lgan har qanday narsadan ajoyib ijobiy va hayratlanarli tarzda ancha oshib ketmoq.
words.example1: The startup's success went beyond their wildest dreams — they became a billion-dollar company in three years.
words.example1Translation: Startapning muvaffaqiyati ularning eng yovvoyi orzularidan ham oshib ketdi — ular uch yil ichida milliard dollarlik kompaniyaga aylandi.
words.example2: Winning the championship was beyond my wildest dreams when I first started playing.
words.example2Translation: Chempionlikni qo'lga kiritish birinchi bor o'yna boshlaganimda eng yovvoyi orzularimdan ham tashqarida edi.
words.example3: The trip to Japan was beyond our wildest dreams — every day brought a new amazing experience.
words.example3Translation: Yaponiyaga sayohat eng yovvoyi orzularimizdan ham tashqarida edi — har kun yangi ajoyib tajriba olib keldi.
extraExamples: Her debut novel sold beyond her wildest dreams, becoming an international bestseller.
The technology we use today would have been beyond the wildest dreams of people fifty years ago.
He achieved wealth beyond his wildest dreams after his invention took off globally.
The film's box office performance was beyond the wildest dreams of its low-budget producers.
Life in the new country turned out to be beyond their wildest dreams.
The support she received after her speech was beyond her wildest dreams.
The team's comeback victory was beyond the wildest dreams of even their most loyal fans.
extraExampleTranslations: Uning ilk romani eng yovvoyi orzularidan ham ko'proq sotildi va xalqaro bestseller bo'ldi.
Bugun biz foydalanadigan texnologiyalar ellik yil oldin yashagan odamlarning eng yovvoyi orzularidan ham tashqarida bo'lardi.
U ixtirosi butun dunyo bo'ylab mashhur bo'lgach, eng yovvoyi orzularidan ham oshib ketadigan boylikka erishdi.
Filmning kassadagi ko'rsatkichi uning past byudjetli ishlab chiqaruvchilarining eng yovvoyi orzularidan ham oshib ketdi.
Yangi mamlakatdagi hayot ularning eng yovvoyi orzularidan ham chiroyliroq bo'lib chiqdi.
U nutqidan keyin olgan qo'llab-quvvatlash uning eng yovvoyi orzularidan ham tashqarida edi.
Jamoaning qaytib kelish g'alabasi hatto eng sodiq muxlislarining eng yovvoyi orzularidan ham oshib ketdi.

words.definition: To achieve the desired or required result effectively, even if the method or tool used is not ideal.
words.definitionUz: Ishlatiladigan usul yoki vosita ideal bo'lmasa ham, kerakli yoki mo'ljallangan natijaga samarali erishmoq.
words.example1: I didn't have professional tools, but a regular screwdriver did the job perfectly.
words.example1Translation: Menda professional asboblar yo'q edi, lekin oddiy vintburchak ishni mukammal bajardi.
words.example2: The medicine isn't the most advanced, but it does the job and relieves the pain.
words.example2Translation: Dori eng ilg'or emas, lekin ishni bajaradi va og'riqni qoldiradi.
words.example3: We needed a temporary fix for the leak, and a bit of tape did the job for now.
words.example3Translation: Bizga oqish uchun vaqtinchalik tuzatish kerak edi va biroz lenta hozircha ishni bajardi.
extraExamples: It's not the fanciest solution, but it does the job and that's all that matters.
She wasn't the most experienced candidate, but her enthusiasm and dedication did the job.
A simple apology did the job — the argument was resolved quickly.
The old laptop is slow, but it does the job for basic word processing.
We were short on staff, but the volunteers really did the job and made the event a success.
A strong cup of coffee in the morning usually does the job when I'm feeling tired.
The cheap paint they used didn't look great, but it did the job of protecting the walls.
extraExampleTranslations: Bu eng hashamatli yechim emas, lekin ishni bajaradi va bu eng muhim narsa.
U eng tajribali nomzod emas edi, lekin uning ishtiyoqi va bag'ishlanganligi ishni bajardi.
Oddiy uzr so'rash ishni bajardi — bahs tezda hal bo'ldi.
Eski noutbuk sekin, lekin oddiy matn tahrirlash uchun ishni bajaradi.
Xodimlar yetishmasdi, lekin ko'ngillilar haqiqatan ishni bajarib, tadbirni muvaffaqiyatli qildi.
Ertalab kuchli bir finjon qahva charchagan paytimda odatda ishni bajaradi.
Ular ishlagan arzon bo'yoq ajoyib ko'rinmadi, lekin devorlarni himoya qilish ishini bajardi.

words.definition: Based on first impressions or the most obvious interpretation of something, before looking more closely at the details.
words.definitionUz: Tafsilotlarga yaqinroq qarishdan oldin biror narsaning birinchi taassurotlari yoki eng aniq talqiniga asoslangan holda.
words.example1: On the face of it, the deal seemed too good to be true — and it was.
words.example1Translation: Ko'rinishiga ko'ra, shartnoma haqiqat bo'lishi uchun juda yaxshi ko'rindi — va shunday bo'ldi.
words.example2: On the face of it, the two candidates seem very similar, but their policies are quite different.
words.example2Translation: Ko'rinishiga ko'ra, ikki nomzod juda o'xshash ko'rinadi, lekin ularning siyosati juda farqli.
words.example3: On the face of it, skipping sleep to study more seems logical, but research says otherwise.
words.example3Translation: Ko'rinishiga ko'ra, ko'proq o'qish uchun uyquni o'tkazib yuborish mantiqiy ko'rinadi, lekin tadqiqotlar boshqacha aytadi.
extraExamples: On the face of it, the company was doing well, but the accounts told a very different story.
On the face of it, their friendship looked perfect, but there were serious tensions beneath the surface.
The proposal, on the face of it, sounds reasonable — but let's examine it more carefully.
On the face of it, moving abroad for work seemed exciting, but the reality was more complicated.
On the face of it, the new law appeared to protect consumers, but experts found several loopholes.
On the face of it, the painting looked authentic, but closer analysis revealed it was a forgery.
On the face of it, cutting costs seems like the obvious solution, but it can harm quality.
extraExampleTranslations: Ko'rinishiga ko'ra, kompaniya yaxshi ish qilayotgan edi, lekin hisoblar butunlay boshqacha manzarani ko'rsatdi.
Ko'rinishiga ko'ra, ularning do'stligi mukammal ko'rindi, lekin sirt ostida jiddiy keskinliklar bor edi.
Taklif, ko'rinishiga ko'ra, oqilona eshitiladi — lekin uni yanada diqqat bilan ko'rib chiqaylik.
Ko'rinishiga ko'ra, ish uchun chet elga ko'chish qiziqarli ko'rindi, lekin haqiqat yanada murakkab edi.
Ko'rinishiga ko'ra, yangi qonun iste'molchilarni himoya qilgan ko'rindi, lekin mutaxassislar bir nechta bo'shliqlarni topdi.
Ko'rinishiga ko'ra, rasm haqiqiy ko'rindi, lekin yaqinroq tahlil u soxtaligini aniqladi.
Ko'rinishiga ko'ra, xarajatlarni qisqartirish aniq yechim bo'lib ko'rinadi, lekin u sifatga zarar yetkazishi mumkin.

words.definition: To feel tense, nervous, irritable, or anxious, often because of uncertainty or an anticipated difficult situation.
words.definitionUz: Ko'pincha noaniqlik yoki kutilayotgan qiyin vaziyat sababli taranglangan, asabiy, bezovta yoki xavotirli his qilmoq.
words.example1: She was on edge all morning waiting for her exam results to come through.
words.example1Translation: U imtihon natijalarini kutib butun ertalab asabiylashgan holda bo'ldi.
words.example2: The team was on edge before the big presentation to the company's board of directors.
words.example2Translation: Jamoa kompaniyaning boshqaruv kengashiga katta taqdimotdan oldin taranglikda edi.
words.example3: Living near the construction site kept everyone on edge with the constant noise and vibrations.
words.example3Translation: Qurilish maydoni yaqinida yashash doimiy shovqin va titrashlar bilan hammani asabiylashgan holda ushlab turdi.
extraExamples: He was on edge after receiving an unexpected call from his doctor.
The uncertain economic situation has kept many business owners on edge for months.
She tends to be on edge in large crowds, which is why she avoids busy places.
The sudden silence in the building put everyone on edge — something felt wrong.
Being on edge constantly is exhausting and can seriously affect your mental health.
