Xavf biznesning ajralmas qismidir.
Musiqa uning hayotining ajralmas qismidir.
Qiziqish o‘rganishning ajralmas qismidir.
Hurmat rahbarlikning ajralmas qismidir.
O‘zgarish o‘sishning ajralmas qismidir.
His-tuyg‘ular inson bo‘lishning ajralmas qismidir.

words.definition: An achievement that is impressive and requires great skill or effort.
words.definitionUz: Ajoyib yutuq — katta mahorat yoki kuch talab qiladigan ta’sirchan natija.
words.example1: Climbing Mount Everest is a remarkable feat.
words.example1Translation: Everest tog‘iga chiqish ajoyib yutuqdir.
words.example2: Winning the championship was a remarkable feat for the young team.
words.example2Translation: Yosh jamoa uchun chempionlikni yutish ajoyib yutuq edi.
words.example3: Her recovery from the accident was a remarkable feat.
words.example3Translation: Avtohalokatdan tuzalishi ajoyib yutuq edi.
extraExamples: Building the bridge was a remarkable feat of engineering.
He accomplished a remarkable feat at a young age.
The scientist’s discovery was a remarkable feat.
Running a marathon is a remarkable feat.
It was a remarkable feat of bravery.
The rescue operation was a remarkable feat.
Completing the project on time was a remarkable feat.
extraExampleTranslations: Ko‘prikni qurish muhandislikning ajoyib yutug‘i edi.
U yoshligida ajoyib yutuqqa erishdi.
Olimning kashfiyoti ajoyib yutuq edi.
Marafon yugurish ajoyib yutuqdir.
Bu jasoratning ajoyib yutug‘i edi.
Qutqaruv operatsiyasi ajoyib yutuq edi.
Loyihani vaqtida tugatish ajoyib yutuq edi.

words.definition: Elegant, controlled, and polite way of behaving.
words.definitionUz: Naqis xulq — nafis, nazoratli va odobli xulq-atvor.
words.example1: She handled the difficult situation with a graceful manner.
words.example1Translation: U qiyin vaziyatni naqis xulq bilan hal qildi.
words.example2: His graceful manner impressed everyone at the meeting.
words.example2Translation: Uning naqis xulqi yig‘ilishdagi hammani hayratda qoldirdi.
words.example3: The dancer moved with graceful manner.
words.example3Translation: Raqqosa naqis xulq bilan harakat qilardi.
extraExamples: She accepted the criticism with graceful manner.
His graceful manner made him popular.
The hostess welcomed guests with graceful manner.
Graceful manner is important in diplomacy.
She has a naturally graceful manner.
Even in defeat, he behaved with graceful manner.
The teacher spoke with graceful manner.
extraExampleTranslations: U tanqidni naqis xulq bilan qabul qildi.
Uning naqis xulqi uni mashhur qildi.
Uy egasi mehmonlarni naqis xulq bilan kutib oldi.
Diplomatiyada naqis xulq muhim.
U tabiatan naqis xulqqa ega.
Mag‘lubiyatda ham u naqis xulq bilan o‘zini tutdi.
O‘qituvchi naqis xulq bilan gapirdi.

words.definition: A person who is more focused on internal thoughts and feelings and prefers less social interaction.
words.definitionUz: Introvert — ichki fikr va his-tuyg‘ularga ko‘proq e’tibor beradigan va ijtimoiy muloqotni kamroq afzal ko‘radigan odam.
words.example1: As an introvert, she enjoys spending time alone.
words.example1Translation: Introvert sifatida u yolg‘iz vaqt o‘tkazishni yaxshi ko‘radi.
words.example2: Introverts often think deeply before speaking.
words.example2Translation: Introvertlar gapirishdan oldin chuqur o‘ylashadi.
words.example3: Many successful writers are introverts.
words.example3Translation: Ko‘plab muvaffaqiyatli yozuvchilar introvertlardir.
extraExamples: He is a quiet introvert who loves reading.
Introverts recharge by spending time alone.
Being an introvert is not a weakness.
The party was too much for the introvert.
Introverts can be very creative.
She identifies as an introvert.
Society sometimes misunderstands introverts.
extraExampleTranslations: U kitob o‘qishni yaxshi ko‘radigan jim introvert.
Introvertlar yolg‘iz vaqt o‘tkazish orqali kuch to‘playdi.
Introvert bo‘lish zaiflik emas.
Ziyofat introvert uchun juda ko‘p edi.
Introvertlar juda ijodkor bo‘lishi mumkin.
U o‘zini introvert deb hisoblaydi.
Jamiyat ba’zida introvertlarni noto‘g‘ri tushunadi.

words.definition: To deal with a difficult or unpleasant problem firmly and directly.
words.definitionUz: Qiyin masalani hal qilish — qiyin yoki yoqimsiz muammoni qat’iy va to‘g‘ridan-to‘g‘ri hal qilish.
words.example1: It’s time to grasp the nettle and make a decision.
words.example1Translation: Qaror qabul qilib, qiyin masalani hal qilish vaqti keldi.
words.example2: The manager grasped the nettle and fired the underperforming employee.
words.example2Translation: Rahbar qiyin masalani hal qilib, yomon ishlayotgan xodimni ishdan bo‘shatdi.
words.example3: She grasped the nettle and confronted her fears.
words.example3Translation: U qo‘rquvlarini yengish uchun qiyin masalani hal qildi.
extraExamples: We need to grasp the nettle and address the budget problem.
He finally grasped the nettle and apologized.
Grasping the nettle is necessary for progress.
The government must grasp the nettle on climate change.
She grasped the nettle by moving to a new city.
It’s better to grasp the nettle early.
Leaders must grasp the nettle in difficult times.
extraExampleTranslations: Byudjet muammosini hal qilishimiz kerak.
U nihoyat qiyin masalani hal qilib, kechirim so‘radi.
Qiyin masalani hal qilish taraqqiyot uchun zarur.
Hukumat iqlim o‘zgarishi bo‘yicha qiyin masalani hal qilishi kerak.
U yangi shaharga ko‘chib, qiyin masalani hal qildi.
Qiyin masalani erta hal qilgan yaxshi.
Rahbarlar qiyin paytlarda qiyin masalalarni hal qilishlari kerak.

words.definition: A weak and unconvincing reason given to justify something.
words.definitionUz: Zaif bahona — biror narsani oqlash uchun berilgan zaif va ishonarli bo‘lmagan sabab.
words.example1: His feeble excuse didn’t convince anyone.
words.example1Translation: Uning zaif bahonasi hech kimni ishontirmadi.
words.example2: She gave a feeble excuse for being late.
words.example2Translation: U kechikishi uchun zaif bahona keltirdi.
words.example3: Stop making feeble excuses and take responsibility.
words.example3Translation: Zaif bahonalarni to‘xtating va mas’uliyatni o‘zingizga oling.
extraExamples: That’s a very feeble excuse.
The student’s feeble excuse made the teacher angry.
He always comes up with feeble excuses.
Feeble excuses won’t help you.
Her feeble excuse was quickly seen through.
Don’t accept feeble excuses.
The boss rejected his feeble excuse.
extraExampleTranslations: Bu juda zaif bahona.
Talabaning zaif bahonasi o‘qituvchini g‘azablantirdi.
U har doim zaif bahonalar topadi.
Zaif bahonalar sizga yordam bermaydi.
Uning zaif bahonasi tezda fosh bo‘ldi.
Zaif bahonalarni qabul qilmang.
Rahbar uning zaif bahonasini rad etdi.

words.definition: Unemployed; without work.
words.definitionUz: Ishsiz qolish — ishsiz qolgan holat.
words.example1: He has been out of a job for six months.
words.example1Translation: U olti oydan beri ishsiz.
words.example2: Many people found themselves out of a job after the factory closed.
words.example2Translation: Zavod yopilgandan keyin ko‘plab odamlar ishsiz qolishdi.
words.example3: She is currently out of a job but looking for new opportunities.
words.example3Translation: U hozir ishsiz, lekin yangi imkoniyatlarni izlamoqda.
extraExamples: Being out of a job is stressful.
He took the news of being out of a job calmly.
Thousands were left out of a job.
She felt lost when she was out of a job.
It’s hard to be out of a job for a long time.
Many graduates start out of a job.
He’s been out of a job since the pandemic.
extraExampleTranslations: Ishsiz qolish stressli.
U ishsiz qolganini xotirjam qabul qildi.
Minglab odamlar ishsiz qolishdi.
Ishsiz qolganda u o‘zini yo‘qotgandek his qildi.
Uzun muddat ishsiz qolish qiyin.
Ko‘plab bitiruvchilar dastlab ishsiz bo‘lishadi.
U pandemiyadan beri ishsiz.

words.definition: To enter or interfere in a situation or place without permission.
words.definitionUz: Bezovta qilmoq — ruxsatsiz biror joyga kirish yoki vaziyatga aralashish.
words.example1: I’m sorry to intrude, but I need to speak with you.
words.example1Translation: Bezovta qilganim uchun uzr, lekin siz bilan gaplashishim kerak.
words.example2: Please don’t intrude on their private conversation.
words.example2Translation: Iltimos, ularning shaxsiy suhbatiga aralashmang.
words.example3: The reporter tried to intrude into the meeting.
words.example3Translation: Jurnalist yig‘ilishga aralashishga harakat qildi.
extraExamples: It’s rude to intrude without knocking.
She didn’t want to intrude on their family time.
The noise intruded on my concentration.
He felt he was intruding on their privacy.
Don’t intrude in other people’s affairs.
The sunlight intruded through the curtains.
I hate to intrude, but this is urgent.
extraExampleTranslations: Taqillatmasdan kirish qo‘pollikdir.
U ularning oilaviy vaqtiga aralashishni xohlamadi.
Shovqin mening diqqatimni buzdi.
U ularning shaxsiy hayotiga aralashayotgandek his qildi.
Boshqalarning ishlariga aralashmang.
Quyosh nuri pardalardan o‘tib kirdi.
Bezovta qilishni yoqtirmayman, lekin bu shoshilinch.

words.definition: Immediately accessible or readily available.
words.definitionUz: Qo‘lingizda — darhol foydalanish mumkin bo‘lgan yoki osonlikcha mavjud.
words.example1: All the information you need is at your fingertips.
words.example1Translation: Sizga kerak bo‘lgan barcha ma’lumotlar qo‘lingizda.
words.example2: With smartphones, knowledge is at your fingertips.
words.example2Translation: Smartfonlar bilan bilim qo‘lingizda.
words.example3: The answers are at your fingertips if you search online.
words.example3Translation: Internetda qidirib topsangiz, javoblar qo‘lingizda.
extraExamples: Technology puts the world at your fingertips.
She has all the tools at her fingertips.
Help is at your fingertips.
The library puts knowledge at your fingertips.
With this app, everything is at your fingertips.
He always has the facts at his fingertips.
Success is at your fingertips if you work hard.
extraExampleTranslations: Texnologiya butun dunyoni qo‘lingizga beradi.
Uning barcha asboblari qo‘l ostida.
Yordam qo‘lingizda.
Kutubxona bilimni qo‘lingizga beradi.
Bu ilova bilan hamma narsa qo‘lingizda.
U har doim faktlarni qo‘l ostida saqlaydi.
Qattiq mehnat qilsangiz, muvaffaqiyat qo‘lingizda.

words.definition: Guided by instinct or feeling rather than conscious logical reasoning.
words.definitionUz: Intuitsiyaga asoslangan — mantiqiy fikrlashdan ko‘ra instinkt yoki his-tuyg‘uga asoslangan.
words.example1: Her decision was based on intuition.
words.example1Translation: Uning qarori intuitsiyaga asoslangan edi.
words.example2: He trusted his intuition and made the right choice.
words.example2Translation: U intuitsiyasiga ishonib, to‘g‘ri tanlov qildi.
words.example3: Many great discoveries are based on intuition.
words.example3Translation: Ko‘plab buyuk kashfiyotlar intuitsiyaga asoslangan.
extraExamples: The detective solved the case based on intuition.
Sometimes decisions based on intuition are best.
Her success was based on intuition and hard work.
He acted based on intuition rather than data.
Intuition-based choices can be surprisingly accurate.
The artist created the painting based on intuition.
She relies on intuition in difficult situations.
extraExampleTranslations: Tergovchi ishni intuitsiyaga asoslanib hal qildi.
Ba’zida intuitsiyaga asoslangan qarorlar eng yaxshisi bo‘ladi.
Uning muvaffaqiyati intuitsiya va qattiq mehnatga asoslangan edi.
U ma’lumotlardan ko‘ra intuitsiyasiga asoslanib harakat qildi.
Intuitsiyaga asoslangan tanlovlar hayratlanarli darajada to‘g‘ri bo‘lishi mumkin.
Rassom rasmni intuitsiyasiga asoslanib chizdi.
U qiyin vaziyatlarda intuitsiyasiga tayanadi.

words.definition: Information that is deeply unpleasant, depressing, or distressing.
words.definitionUz: Achchiq xabar — juda yoqimsiz, tushkunlikka soladigan yoki og‘ir xabar.
words.example1: The doctor delivered grim news about her condition.
words.example1Translation: Shifokor uning ahvoli haqida achchiq xabar berdi.
words.example2: We received grim news about the accident.
words.example2Translation: Biz avtohalokat haqida achchiq xabar oldik.
words.example3: The grim news shocked the entire community.
words.example3Translation: Achchiq xabar butun jamoani hayratda qoldirdi.
extraExamples: The grim news spread quickly.
He tried to prepare her for the grim news.
Grim news about the economy worried everyone.
The family received grim news from the hospital.
Despite the grim news, she remained hopeful.
The report contained grim news about climate change.
It was a day of grim news.
extraExampleTranslations: Achchiq xabar tez tarqaldi.
U uni achchiq xabarga tayyorlashga harakat qildi.
Iqtisodiyot haqidagi achchiq xabar hammani xavotirga soldi.
Oila shifoxonadan achchiq xabar oldi.
Achchiq xabarga qaramay, u umidini yo‘qotmadi.
Hisobotda iqlim o‘zgarishi haqida achchiq xabar bor edi.
Bu kun achchiq xabarlar kuni edi.

words.definition: To intrude into someone’s personal life without permission.
words.definitionUz: Shaxsiy hayotiga aralashish — ruxsatsiz kimningdir shaxsiy hayotiga kirish yoki aralashish.
words.example1: Reading her diary would invade her privacy.
words.example1Translation: Kundalikni o‘qish uning shaxsiy hayotiga aralashish bo‘ladi.
words.example2: The media invaded the celebrity’s privacy.
words.example2Translation: Ommaviy axborot vositalari mashhurning shaxsiy hayotiga aralashdi.
words.example3: It’s important not to invade someone’s privacy.
words.example3Translation: Boshqalarning shaxsiy hayotiga aralashmaslik muhim.
extraExamples: Installing cameras everywhere invades privacy.
Don’t invade my privacy!
The government was accused of invading citizens’ privacy.
Social media can invade privacy.
He felt his privacy was being invaded.
Laws protect against invading privacy.
Asking too many personal questions invades privacy.
extraExampleTranslations: Hamma joyga kameralar o‘rnatish shaxsiy hayotga aralashishdir.
Mening shaxsiy hayotimga aralashma!
Hukumat fuqarolarning shaxsiy hayotiga aralashishda ayblandi.
Ijtimoiy tarmoqlar shaxsiy hayotga aralashishi mumkin.
U o‘zining shaxsiy hayotiga aralashilayotganini his qildi.
Qonunlar shaxsiy hayotga aralashishdan himoya qiladi.
Juda ko‘p shaxsiy savollar berish shaxsiy hayotga aralashishdir.

words.definition: To do the same thing as someone else has just done.
words.definitionUz: O‘xshab harakat qilish — boshqa birov qilgan ishni o‘zi ham qilish.
words.example1: When the leader stood up, everyone followed suit.
words.example1Translation: Rahbar o‘rnidan turgach, hamma unga ergashdi.
words.example2: If one company raises prices, others usually follow suit.
words.example2Translation: Agar bir kompaniya narxlarni oshirsa, boshqalari ham odatda shunday qiladi.
words.example3: She decided to follow suit and apply for the scholarship.
words.example3Translation: U stipendiyaga hujjat topshirib, o‘xshab harakat qilishga qaror qildi.
extraExamples: The other players followed suit after the first goal.
When one country imposed sanctions, several others followed suit.
He bought a new car, and his brother followed suit.
Many restaurants followed suit and added vegan options.
If you apologize first, others may follow suit.
The team started celebrating, and the fans followed suit.
She invested in tech stocks, and her friends followed suit.
extraExampleTranslations: Birinchi gol urilgandan keyin boshqa o‘yinchilar ham shunday qilishdi.
Bir mamlakat sanksiyalar qo‘llagach, yana bir nechtasi unga ergashdi.
U yangi mashina sotib oldi, ukasi ham unga ergashdi.
Ko‘plab restoranlar unga ergashib, vegan taomlarni qo‘shishdi.
Agar siz birinchi bo‘lib kechirim so‘rasangiz, boshqalar ham shunday qilishi mumkin.
Jamoa bayram qila boshlagach, muxlislar ham shunday qilishdi.
U texnologiya aksiyalariga sarmoya kiritdi, do‘stlari ham unga ergashishdi.

words.definition: Always or almost always; without exception.
words.definitionUz: Doim — har doim yoki deyarli har doim; istisnosiz.
words.example1: He is invariably late for meetings.
words.example1Translation: U yig‘ilishlarga doim kechikadi.
words.example2: The train is invariably delayed on this route.
words.example2Translation: Bu yo‘nalishda poyezd doim kechikadi.
words.example3: She invariably chooses the healthiest option.
words.example3Translation: U doim eng sog‘lom variantni tanlaydi.
extraExamples: It invariably rains when we plan a picnic.
He invariably forgets his keys.
The results are invariably positive.
She invariably supports her friends.
The boss is invariably in a good mood on Fridays.
This method invariably works.
Children invariably ask difficult questions.
extraExampleTranslations: Piknik rejalashtirganimizda doim yomg‘ir yog‘adi.
U doim kalitlarini unutadi.
Natijalar doim ijobiy.
U doim do‘stlarini qo‘llab-quvvatlaydi.
Rahbar juma kunlari doim yaxshi kayfiyatda bo‘ladi.
Bu usul doim ishlaydi.
Bolalar doim qiyin savollar berishadi.

words.definition: To endure something unpleasant or painful without complaining.
words.definitionUz: Tishini qisib chidash — yoqimsiz yoki og‘riqli narsaga shikoyat qilmasdan chidash.
words.example1: The work is boring, but I just grin and bear it.
words.example1Translation: Ish zerikarli, lekin men tishimni qisib chidayman.
words.example2: She grinned and bore it during the long meeting.
words.example2Translation: U uzoq yig‘ilish davomida tishini qisib chidadi.
words.example3: Sometimes you just have to grin and bear it.
words.example3Translation: Ba’zida tishni qisib chidashdan boshqa iloj yo‘q.
extraExamples: He grinned and bore the pain after the injury.
We had to grin and bear the bad weather.
She grinned and bore her boss’s criticism.
Grin and bear it until the end of the project.
They grinned and bore the long flight.
I’m tired of grinning and bearing it.
The team grinned and bore the difficult training.
extraExampleTranslations: U jarohatdan keyin og‘riqqa tishini qisib chidadi.
Biz yomon ob-havoga tish qisib chidashimiz kerak edi.
U rahbarining tanqidiga tishini qisib chidadi.
Loyiha tugaguncha tish qisib chida.
Ular uzoq parvozga tish qisib chidashdi.
Tish qisib chidashdan charchadim.
Jamoa qiyin mashg‘ulotga tish qisib chidadi.

words.definition: To stimulate someone’s creativity, interest, or enthusiasm.
words.definitionUz: Tasavvurini uyg‘otmoq — kimningdir ijodkorligi, qiziqishi yoki ishtiyoqini kuchaytirish.
words.example1: The book fired my imagination.
words.example1Translation: Kitob mening tasavvurimni uyg‘otdi.
words.example2: Space exploration fires the imagination of young people.
words.example2Translation: Kosmik tadqiqotlar yoshlarning tasavvurini uyg‘otadi.
words.example3: Her speech fired the audience’s imagination.
words.example3Translation: Uning nutqi tomoshabinlarning tasavvurini uyg‘otdi.
extraExamples: The movie really fired my imagination.
New technologies fire the imagination of scientists.
Stories of adventure fire children’s imagination.
The painting fired her imagination.
Music can fire the imagination.
The teacher’s lesson fired the students’ imagination.
Traveling to new places always fires my imagination.
extraExampleTranslations: Film mening tasavvurimni juda uyg‘otdi.
Yangi texnologiyalar olimlarning tasavvurini uyg‘otadi.
Sarguzasht hikoyalari bolalarning tasavvurini uyg‘otadi.
Rasm uning tasavvurini uyg‘otdi.
Musiqa tasavvurni uyg‘otishi mumkin.
O‘qituvchining darsi talabalar tasavvurini uyg‘otdi.
Yangi joylarga sayohat har doim mening tasavvurimni uyg‘otadi.

words.definition: To make a deep sound expressing severe physical pain.
words.definitionUz: Og‘riqdan ingramoq — kuchli jismoniy og‘riqdan chuqur ovoz chiqarish.
words.example1: He groaned with pain after falling off the ladder.
words.example1Translation: Naryadadan yiqilgandan keyin u og‘riqdan ingradi.
words.example2: The patient groaned with pain as the doctor examined him.
words.example2Translation: Shifokor uni ko‘zdan kechirayotganda bemor og‘riqdan ingradi.
words.example3: She tried not to groan with pain in front of the children.
words.example3Translation: U bolalar oldida og‘riqdan ingramaslikka harakat qildi.
extraExamples: The injured player groaned with pain on the field.
He groaned with pain every time he moved.
The dog groaned with pain after the accident.
She groaned with pain but didn’t cry.
Groaning with pain is a natural reaction.
The old man groaned with pain as he stood up.
The soldier groaned with pain but continued fighting.
extraExampleTranslations: Jarohatlangan o‘yinchi maydonda og‘riqdan ingradi.
U har harakat qilganda og‘riqdan ingrardi.
It avtohalokatdan keyin og‘riqdan ingradi.
U og‘riqdan ingradi, lekin yig‘lamadi.
Og‘riqdan ingrash tabiiy reaktsiya.
Keksa odam o‘rnidan turganda og‘riqdan ingradi.
Askar og‘riqdan ingrab, jangni davom ettirdi.

words.definition: An act that violates someone’s personal boundaries or private life.
words.definitionUz: Shaxsiy hayotga tajovuz — kimningdir shaxsiy chegaralariga yoki maxfiy hayotiga ruxsatsiz aralashish.
words.example1: Taking photos without permission is an invasion of privacy.
words.example1Translation: Ruxsatsiz suratga olish shaxsiy hayotga tajovuzdir.
words.example2: The newspaper article was an invasion of her privacy.
words.example2Translation: Gazetadagi maqola uning shaxsiy hayotiga tajovuz edi.
words.example3: Installing hidden cameras is a serious invasion of privacy.
words.example3Translation: Yashirin kameralar o‘rnatish shaxsiy hayotga jiddiy tajovuzdir.
extraExamples: Reading someone’s messages is an invasion of privacy.
The law protects against invasion of privacy.
Social media can lead to invasion of privacy.
He felt it was an invasion of his privacy.
The investigation became an invasion of privacy.
We must respect others and avoid invasion of privacy.
The celebrity complained about the invasion of privacy.
extraExampleTranslations: Biровning xabarlarini o‘qish shaxsiy hayotga tajovuzdir.
Qonun shaxsiy hayotga tajovuzdan himoya qiladi.
Ijtimoiy tarmoqlar shaxsiy hayotga tajovuzga olib kelishi mumkin.
U buni o‘zining shaxsiy hayotiga tajovuz deb his qildi.
Tergov shaxsiy hayotga tajovuzga aylandi.
Boshqalarni hurmat qilishimiz va shaxsiy hayotga tajovuz qilmasligimiz kerak.
Mashhur shaxs shaxsiy hayotiga tajovuzdan shikoyat qildi.

words.definition: To understand and accept a difficult or unpleasant situation.
words.definitionUz: Qabul qilish va tushunish — qiyin yoki yoqimsiz vaziyatni tushunib, qabul qilish.
words.example1: It took him a long time to come to grips with the loss.
words.example1Translation: Yo‘qotishni qabul qilish va tushunish uchun unga uzoq vaqt kerak bo‘ldi.
words.example2: She is still coming to grips with the diagnosis.
words.example2Translation: U hali ham tashxisni qabul qilish va tushunish jarayonida.
words.example3: We need to come to grips with reality.
words.example3Translation: Biz haqiqatni qabul qilishimiz kerak.
extraExamples: He finally came to grips with his failure.
It’s hard to come to grips with such big changes.
The country is coming to grips with the crisis.
She came to grips with her past mistakes.
Coming to grips with the truth was painful.
The team came to grips with their poor performance.
I’m slowly coming to grips with the new situation.
extraExampleTranslations: U nihoyat o‘z muvaffaqiyatsizligini qabul qildi.
Bunday katta o‘zgarishlarni qabul qilish qiyin.
Mamlakat inqirozni qabul qilish va tushunish jarayonida.
U o‘tmishdagi xatolarini qabul qildi.
Haqiqatni qabul qilish og‘riqli bo‘ldi.
Jamoa o‘zining yomon natijasini qabul qildi.
Men yangi vaziyatni asta-sekin qabul qilyapman.

words.definition: Happening in a way that is contrary to what is expected, often in an amusing or strange way.
words.definitionUz: Kinoyali — kutilganidan butunlay teskari tarzda sodir bo‘ladigan, ko‘pincha kulgili yoki g‘alati holat.
words.example1: It’s ironic that the fire station burned down.
words.example1Translation: O‘t o‘chirish stansiyasining yonib ketishi kinoyali holat.
words.example2: The ironic thing is that he won by accident.
words.example2Translation: Kinoyali tomoni shundaki, u tasodifan g‘alaba qozondi.
words.example3: She found it ironic that the critic’s own book failed.
words.example3Translation: Tanqidchining o‘z kitobi muvaffaqiyatsiz bo‘lgani unga kinoyali tuyuldi.
extraExamples: It’s ironic how the richest man complains about money.
The ironic ending surprised everyone.
He smiled with ironic amusement.
The situation became increasingly ironic.
It’s ironic that the expert made such a basic mistake.
Life is full of ironic twists.
She gave an ironic laugh.
extraExampleTranslations: Eng boy odamning pul haqida shikoyat qilishi kinoyali.
Kinoyali oxiri hammani hayratda qoldirdi.
U kinoyali tabassum bilan jilmaydi.
Vaziyat tobora kinoyali bo‘lib bordi.
Mutaxassisning bunchalik oddiy xato qilgani kinoyali.
Hayot kinoyali burilishlarga to‘la.
U kinoyali kulgi bilan kuldi.

words.definition: To maintain one’s position or opinion despite opposition or pressure.
words.definitionUz: O‘z pozitsiyasida turish — qarshilik yoki bosimga qaramay o‘z fikri yoki pozitsiyasida qat’iy turish.
words.example1: You need to stand your ground and say no.
words.example1Translation: Siz o‘z pozitsiyangizda turib, “yo‘q” deyishingiz kerak.
words.example2: She stood her ground during the argument.
words.example2Translation: U bahs paytida o‘z pozitsiyasida turdi.
words.example3: It’s important to stand your ground on important issues.
words.example3Translation: Muhim masalalarda o‘z pozitsiyangizda turish muhim.
extraExamples: The team stood their ground and won the match.
He stood his ground against unfair criticism.
Standing your ground shows strength.
Don’t be afraid to stand your ground.
She stood her ground and refused the offer.
The protesters stood their ground.
Leaders must stand their ground on principles.
extraExampleTranslations: Jamoa o‘z pozitsiyasida turib, o‘yinni yutdi.
U adolatsiz tanqidga qarshi o‘z pozitsiyasida turdi.
O‘z pozitsiyangizda turish kuchlilikni ko‘rsatadi.
O‘z pozitsiyangizda turishdan qo‘rqmang.
U o‘z pozitsiyasida turib, taklifni rad etdi.
Namoyishchilar o‘z pozitsiyalarida turishdi.
Rahbarlar printsiplar bo‘yicha o‘z pozitsiyalarida turishlari kerak.

words.definition: To take care of yourself without help from others.
words.definitionUz: O‘zingni o‘zing boqish — boshqalarning yordamisiz o‘zingga o‘zing g‘amxo‘rlik qilish.
words.example1: When I was young, I had to fend for myself.
words.example1Translation: Yoshligimda o‘zimni o‘zim boqishimga to‘g‘ri keldi.
words.example2: After the parents left, the children had to fend for themselves.
words.example2Translation: Ota-onalar ketgach, bolalar o‘zlarini o‘zlari boqishga majbur bo‘lishdi.
words.example3: It’s time you learned to fend for yourself.
words.example3Translation: O‘zingni o‘zing boqishni o‘rganish vaqti keldi.
extraExamples: In the wild, animals must fend for themselves.
She learned to fend for herself at an early age.
You can’t always rely on others — you need to fend for yourself.
He was left to fend for himself after the divorce.
Fending for yourself builds independence.
The course teaches students to fend for themselves.
During the trip, we had to fend for ourselves.
extraExampleTranslations: Yovvoyi tabiatda hayvonlar o‘zlarini o‘zlari boqishlari kerak.
U erta yoshda o‘zini o‘zi boqishni o‘rgangan.
Har doim boshqalarga tayanib bo‘lmaydi — o‘zingni o‘zing boqishing kerak.
Ajralishdan keyin u o‘zini o‘zi boqishga majbur bo‘ldi.
O‘zingni o‘zing boqish mustaqillikni rivojlantiradi.
Kurs talabalarga o‘zlarini o‘zlari boqishni o‘rgatadi.
Sayohat paytida biz o‘zimizni o‘zimiz boqishimiz kerak edi.

words.definition: Soil rich in nutrients that is good for growing plants; also used figuratively for favourable conditions.
words.definitionUz: Unumdor tuproq — o‘simliklar o‘sishi uchun zarur oziq moddalar bilan boy tuproq; majoziy ma’noda qulay sharoitlarni ham bildiradi.
words.example1: The farmers planted crops in the fertile soil.
words.example1Translation: Dehqonlar unumdor tuproqqa ekin ekishdi.
words.example2: The city provides fertile soil for new businesses.
words.example2Translation: Shahar yangi bizneslar uchun qulay sharoit yaratadi.
words.example3: This environment is fertile soil for creativity.
words.example3Translation: Bu muhit ijodkorlik uchun unumdor tuproqdir.
extraExamples: Fertile soil is essential for agriculture.
The region has very fertile soil.
The book created fertile soil for discussion.
Her mind is fertile soil for new ideas.
Pollution is destroying fertile soil.
The school provides fertile soil for young talents.
Economic stability creates fertile soil for growth.
extraExampleTranslations: Unumdor tuproq qishloq xo‘jaligi uchun zarur.
Bu mintaqada juda unumdor tuproq bor.
Kitob muhokama uchun unumdor tuproq yaratdi.
Uning ongida yangi g‘oyalar uchun unumdor tuproq bor.
If loslanish unumdor tuproqni yo‘q qilmoqda.
Maktab yosh iste’dodlar uchun unumdor tuproq yaratadi.
Iqtisodiy barqarorlik o‘sish uchun unumdor tuproq yaratadi.

words.word: To be itching to do something
words.definition: To feel a strong desire or impatience to do something.
words.definitionUz: Qo‘li qichishmoq — biror ishni qilishga kuchli ishtiyoq yoki sabrsizlik his qilish.
words.example1: The children were itching to open their presents.
words.example1Translation: Bolalarning sovg‘alarni ochishga qo‘llari qichishardi.
words.example2: I’m itching to tell you the news.
words.example2Translation: Sizga yangilikni aytishga qo‘lim qichishmoqda.
words.example3: He was itching to start the new project.
words.example3Translation: U yangi loyihani boshlashga qo‘li qichishardi.
extraExamples: She was itching to show off her new car.
The players were itching to get back on the field.
I’m itching to try the new restaurant.
He’s itching to tell everyone about his success.
The kids are itching to go outside.
She’s itching to start her own business.
We were all itching to hear the results.
extraExampleTranslations: U yangi mashinasini ko‘rsatishga qo‘li qichishardi.
O‘yinchilar maydonga qaytishga qo‘llari qichishardi.
Yangi restoranni sinab ko‘rishga qo‘lim qichishmoqda.
U muvaffaqiyatini hammaga aytishga qo‘li qichishmoqda.
Bolalar tashqariga chiqishga qo‘llari qichishmoqda.
U o‘z biznesini boshlashga qo‘li qichishmoqda.
Hammamiz natijalarni eshitishga qo‘limiz qichishardi.

words.definition: To do something innovative or different that has not been done before.
words.definitionUz: Yangi yo‘l ochmoq — ilgari hech kim qilmagan yangi yoki innovatsion ishni amalga oshirish.
words.example1: The scientist broke new ground in cancer research.
words.example1Translation: Olim saraton tadqiqotlarida yangi yo‘l ochdi.
words.example2: Her book breaks new ground in modern literature.
words.example2Translation: Uning kitobi zamonaviy adabiyotda yangi yo‘l ochadi.
words.example3: The company is breaking new ground with this technology.
words.example3Translation: Kompaniya bu texnologiya bilan yangi yo‘l ochmoqda.
extraExamples: The artist broke new ground with her style.
This research breaks new ground in the field.
They broke new ground by using AI in education.
The expedition broke new ground in exploration.
Her discovery broke new ground for future scientists.
The director broke new ground in filmmaking.
Breaking new ground requires courage.
extraExampleTranslations: Rassom o‘z uslubi bilan yangi yo‘l ochdi.
Bu tadqiqot sohada yangi yo‘l ochadi.
Ular ta’limda sun’iy intellektdan foydalanib yangi yo‘l ochishdi.
Ekspeditsiya tadqiqotlarda yangi yo‘l ochdi.
Uning kashfiyoti kelajak olimlari uchun yangi yo‘l ochdi.
Rejissyor kinematografiyada yangi yo‘l ochdi.
Yangi yo‘l ochish jasoratni talab qiladi.

words.definition: Being discussed or disputed; the main point of disagreement.
words.definitionUz: Muhokama qilinayotgan — hozirda muhokama qilinayotgan yoki bahsli masala; asosiy kelishmovchilik nuqtasi.
words.example1: The point at issue is whether we should continue the project.
words.example1Translation: Muhokama qilinayotgan masala — loyihani davom ettirishimiz kerakmi yoki yo‘qmi.
words.example2: The main issue at issue is funding.
words.example2Translation: Asosiy muhokama qilinayotgan masala — moliyalashtirish.
words.example3: What is at issue here is fairness.
words.example3Translation: Bu yerda muhokama qilinayotgan narsa adolatdir.
extraExamples: The question at issue is very important.
Let’s focus on the matter at issue.
The facts at issue are clear.
There is disagreement on the point at issue.
The problem at issue needs immediate attention.
The rights at issue affect many people.
The topic at issue was education reform.
extraExampleTranslations: Muhokama qilinayotgan savol juda muhim.
Keling, muhokama qilinayotgan masalaga e’tibor qarataylik.
Muhokama qilinayotgan faktlar aniq.
Muhokama qilinayotgan nuqta bo‘yicha kelishmovchilik bor.
Muhokama qilinayotgan muammo darhol e’tibor talab qiladi.
Muhokama qilinayotgan huquqlar ko‘plab odamlarga ta’sir qiladi.
Muhokama qilinayotgan mavzu ta’lim islohoti edi.

words.definition: An invented person in a story, book, movie, or play.
words.definitionUz: Xayoliy qahramon — hikoya, kitob, film yoki spektakldagi ixtiro qilingan shaxs.
words.example1: Harry Potter is a famous fictional character.
words.example1Translation: Garri Potter mashhur xayoliy qahramondir.
words.example2: The author created a complex fictional character.
words.example2Translation: Yozuvchi murakkab xayoliy qahramon yaratdi.
words.example3: Children love fictional characters like superheroes.
words.example3Translation: Bolalar superqahramonlar kabi xayoliy qahramonlarni yaxshi ko‘rishadi.
extraExamples: Sherlock Holmes is one of the most famous fictional characters.
The movie has many interesting fictional characters.
She identifies with the fictional character in the book.
Creating believable fictional characters is an art.
The fictional character became a cultural icon.
Many fictional characters are based on real people.
The story revolves around a fictional character.
extraExampleTranslations: Sherlok Xolms eng mashhur xayoliy qahramonlardan biri.
Filmda ko‘plab qiziqarli xayoliy qahramonlar bor.
U kitobdagi xayoliy qahramon bilan o‘zini bog‘laydi.
Ishonarli xayoliy qahramonlar yaratish san’atdir.
Xayoliy qahramon madaniy timsolga aylandi.
Ko‘plab xayoliy qahramonlar haqiqiy odamlarga asoslangan.
Hikoya xayoliy qahramon atrofida aylanadi.

words.definition: To assert your authority firmly and refuse to change your decision.
words.definitionUz: Qat’iy turib olish — o‘z qaroringizda qat’iy turib, uni o‘zgartirishdan bosh tortish.
words.example1: I had to put my foot down and say no to the extra work.
words.example1Translation: Qo‘shimcha ishga “yo‘q” deyish uchun qat’iy turib olishimga to‘g‘ri keldi.
words.example2: The parents put their foot down about bedtime.
words.example2Translation: Ota-onalar uyqu vaqti haqida qat’iy turib olishdi.
words.example3: She finally put her foot down and ended the relationship.
words.example3Translation: U nihoyat qat’iy turib, munosabatlarni tugatdi.
extraExamples: The boss put his foot down on late arrivals.
It’s time to put your foot down.
She put her foot down and refused to pay.
Parents need to put their foot down sometimes.
He put his foot down about the budget.
The coach put his foot down during training.
I’m going to put my foot down this time.
extraExampleTranslations: Rahbar kechikib kelishlarga qat’iy turib oldi.
Qat’iy turib olish vaqti keldi.
U qat’iy turib, to‘lashdan bosh tortdi.
Ota-onalar ba’zida qat’iy turib olishlari kerak.
U byudjet bo‘yicha qat’iy turib oldi.
Murabbiy mashg‘ulot paytida qat’iy turib oldi.
Bu safar qat’iy turib olaman.

words.definition: For reasons related to health or medical advice.
words.definitionUz: Tibbiy sabablarga ko‘ra — sog‘liq yoki tibbiy tavsiyaga asoslangan sabablarga ko‘ra.
words.example1: He was excused from work on medical grounds.
words.example1Translation: U tibbiy sabablarga ko‘ra ishdan ozod qilindi.
words.example2: The athlete withdrew from the competition on medical grounds.
words.example2Translation: Sportchi tibbiy sabablarga ko‘ra musobaqadan chiqdi.
words.example3: She requested leave on medical grounds.
words.example3Translation: U tibbiy sabablarga ko‘ra ta’til so‘radi.
extraExamples: The student was absent on medical grounds.
The surgery was postponed on medical grounds.
He was declared unfit on medical grounds.
The visa was denied on medical grounds.
Many soldiers were discharged on medical grounds.
The decision was made on medical grounds.
She retired early on medical grounds.
extraExampleTranslations: Talaba tibbiy sabablarga ko‘ra darsga kelmadi.
Operatsiya tibbiy sabablarga ko‘ra keyinga qoldirildi.
U tibbiy sabablarga ko‘ra yaroqsiz deb topildi.
Viza tibbiy sabablarga ko‘ra rad etildi.
Ko‘plab askarlar tibbiy sabablarga ko‘ra armiyadan bo‘shatildi.
Qaror tibbiy sabablarga ko‘ra qabul qilindi.
U tibbiy sabablarga ko‘ra erta pensiyaga chiqdi.

words.definition: To be the most advanced, successful, or best in a particular area.
words.definitionUz: Yetakchilik qilmoq — ma’lum bir sohada eng ilg‘or, muvaffaqiyatli yoki eng yaxshi bo‘lish.
words.example1: The company leads the field in renewable energy.
words.example1Translation: Kompaniya qayta tiklanadigan energiya sohasida yetakchilik qiladi.
words.example2: She leads the field in medical research.
words.example2Translation: U tibbiy tadqiqotlarda yetakchilik qiladi.
words.example3: This university leads the field in computer science.
words.example3Translation: Bu universitet kompyuter fanlari bo‘yicha yetakchilik qiladi.
extraExamples: Their team leads the field this season.
The country leads the field in technology innovation.
She continues to lead the field despite challenges.
The new product leads the field in design.
His research leads the field worldwide.
The company aims to lead the field.
This book leads the field in its genre.
extraExampleTranslations: Ularning jamoasi bu mavsumda yetakchilik qilmoqda.
Mamlakat texnologiya innovatsiyalarida yetakchilik qiladi.
U qiyinchiliklarga qaramay yetakchilikni davom ettirmoqda.
Yangi mahsulot dizayn bo‘yicha yetakchilik qiladi.
Uning tadqiqoti butun dunyoda yetakchilik qiladi.
Kompaniya yetakchilik qilishni maqsad qilgan.
Bu kitob o‘z janrida yetakchilik qiladi.

words.definition: A situation in which the outcome is the opposite of what was expected, often in an amusing way.
words.definitionUz: Kinoya — natija kutilganidan butunlay teskari bo‘ladigan, ko‘pincha kulgili holat.
words.example1: The irony is that the fire station burned down.
words.example1Translation: Kinoyasi shundaki, o‘t o‘chirish stansiyasi yonib ketdi.
words.example2: There is a certain irony in the situation.
words.example2Translation: Bu vaziyatda ma’lum bir kinoya bor.
words.example3: The irony of the situation was not lost on her.
words.example3Translation: Vaziyatning kinoyasi uning e’tiboridan chetda qolmadi.
extraExamples: The irony of fate is sometimes cruel.
There’s a deep irony in his success.
The irony was that she became famous for the thing she hated.
Life is full of irony.
He failed to see the irony of the situation.
The story is filled with irony.
It was the ultimate irony.
extraExampleTranslations: Taqdir kinoyasi ba’zida shafqatsiz bo‘ladi.
Uning muvaffaqiyatida chuqur kinoya bor.
U nafratlangan narsasi bilan mashhur bo‘lgani kinoyadir.
Hayot kinoyaga to‘la.
U vaziyatning kinoyasini ko‘rmadi.
Hikoya kinoyaga to‘la.
Bu eng yuqori darajadagi kinoya edi.

words.definition: To relax one’s vigilance or caution, often making oneself vulnerable.
words.definitionUz: Ehtiyotkorlikni pasaytirmoq — hushyorlik yoki ehtiyotkorlikni bo‘shashtirib, o‘zini zaif holatga qo‘yish.
words.example1: She finally let her guard down and trusted him.
words.example1Translation: U nihoyat ehtiyotkorlikni pasaytirib, unga ishonishga qaror qildi.
words.example2: Don’t let your guard down too quickly.
words.example2Translation: Ehtiyotkorlikni tez pasaytirmang.
words.example3: He let his guard down and made a mistake.
words.example3Translation: U ehtiyotkorlikni pasaytirib, xato qildi.
extraExamples: It’s hard to let your guard down after being hurt.
In the game, never let your guard down.
She let her guard down during the conversation.
Letting your guard down can be dangerous.
He rarely lets his guard down.
After the victory, the team let their guard down.
Learning to let your guard down is important in relationships.
extraExampleTranslations: Og‘riqdan keyin ehtiyotkorlikni pasaytirish qiyin.
O‘yinda hech qachon ehtiyotkorlikni pasaytirmang.
U suhbat davomida ehtiyotkorlikni pasaytirdi.
Ehtiyotkorlikni pasaytirish xavfli bo‘lishi mumkin.
U kamdan-kam ehtiyotkorlikni pasaytiradi.
G‘alabadan keyin jamoa ehtiyotkorlikni pasaytirdi.
Munosabatlarda ehtiyotkorlikni pasaytirishni o‘rganish muhim.

words.definition: To give something the highest priority.
words.definitionUz: Birinchi o‘ringa qo‘yish — biror narsani eng muhim deb hisoblab, birinchi o‘ringa qo‘yish.
words.example1: She always puts her family first.
words.example1Translation: U har doim oilasini birinchi o‘ringa qo‘yadi.
words.example2: We need to put safety first.
words.example2Translation: Biz xavfsizlikni birinchi o‘ringa qo‘yishimiz kerak.
words.example3: He put his health first and quit smoking.
words.example3Translation: U sog‘lig‘ini birinchi o‘ringa qo‘yib, chekishni tashladi.
extraExamples: Education should be put first.
The company puts customer satisfaction first.
She puts her career first.
We must put the environment first.
He always puts others first.
Putting quality first leads to success.
The government should put people first.
extraExampleTranslations: Ta’limni birinchi o‘ringa qo‘yish kerak.
Kompaniya mijozlar qoniqishini birinchi o‘ringa qo‘yadi.
U kasbini birinchi o‘ringa qo‘yadi.
Biz atrof-muhitni birinchi o‘ringa qo‘yishimiz kerak.
U har doim boshqalarni birinchi o‘ringa qo‘yadi.
Sifatni birinchi o‘ringa qo‘yish muvaffaqiyatga olib keladi.
Hukumat odamlarni birinchi o‘ringa qo‘yishi kerak.

words.definition: An increasing division or difference between two groups or things.
words.definitionUz: Ortib borayotgan tafovat — ikki guruh yoki narsa o‘rtasidagi farq yoki bo‘linishning kuchayishi.
words.example1: There is a growing gulf between rich and poor.
words.example1Translation: Boy va kambag‘allar o‘rtasidagi tafovat ortib bormoqda.
words.example2: The growing gulf between generations is worrying.
words.example2Translation: Avlodlar o‘rtasidagi ortib borayotgan tafovat tashvishli.
words.example3: A growing gulf between theory and practice exists.
words.example3Translation: Nazariya va amaliyot o‘rtasida ortib borayotgan tafovat mavjud.
extraExamples: The growing gulf between urban and rural areas is a problem.
There is a growing gulf in political opinions.
The growing gulf threatens social stability.
We need to bridge the growing gulf.
The growing gulf in education levels is concerning.
A growing gulf developed between the two countries.
The growing gulf in income is widening.
extraExampleTranslations: Shahar va qishloq o‘rtasidagi ortib borayotgan tafovat muammo.
Siyosiy fikrlar o‘rtasida ortib borayotgan tafovat bor.
Ortib borayotgan tafovat ijtimoiy barqarorlikka tahdid solmoqda.
Ortib borayotgan tafovatni bartaraf etishimiz kerak.
Ta’lim darajalaridagi ortib borayotgan tafovat tashvish uyg‘otadi.
Ikki mamlakat o‘rtasida ortib borayotgan tafovat paydo bo‘ldi.
Daromadlardagi tafovat kengayib bormoqda.

words.definition: To satisfy a requirement or demand.
words.definitionUz: Ehtiyojni qondirmoq — talab yoki ehtiyojni bajarish.
words.example1: This product fills the need for affordable smartphones.
words.example1Translation: Bu mahsulot arzon smartfonlar ehtiyojini qondiradi.
words.example2: The new program fills the need for better training.
words.example2Translation: Yangi dastur yaxshiroq tayyorgarlik ehtiyojini qondiradi.
words.example3: Volunteers help fill the need for assistance.
words.example3Translation: Ko‘ngillilar yordam ehtiyojini qondirishga yordam berishadi.
extraExamples: The charity fills the need for food in poor areas.
This course fills the need for specialized skills.
The app fills the need for quick communication.
We must fill the need for better healthcare.
The book fills the need for accessible information.
New jobs will help fill the need for employment.
Technology often fills the need for efficiency.
extraExampleTranslations: Xayriya tashkiloti kambag‘al hududlarda oziq-ovqat ehtiyojini qondiradi.
Bu kurs maxsus ko‘nikmalar ehtiyojini qondiradi.
Ilova tez muloqot ehtiyojini qondiradi.
Biz yaxshiroq sog‘liqni saqlash ehtiyojini qondirishimiz kerak.
Kitob qulay ma’lumot ehtiyojini qondiradi.
Yangi ish o‘rinlari bandlik ehtiyojini qondirishga yordam beradi.
Texnologiya ko‘pincha samaradorlik ehtiyojini qondiradi.

words.definition: To signal to a taxi driver to stop and pick you up.
words.definitionUz: Taksi to‘xtatmoq — taksi haydovchisiga ishora qilib, to‘xtatib, o‘zingizni olib ketishini so‘rash.
words.example1: We hailed a taxi to get to the airport.
words.example1Translation: Biz aeroportga borish uchun taksi to‘xtatdik.
words.example2: It’s hard to hail a taxi during rush hour.
words.example2Translation: Pik soatda taksi to‘xtatish qiyin.
words.example3: She hailed a taxi and jumped in.
words.example3Translation: U taksini to‘xtatib, ichiga sakradi.
extraExamples: He hailed a taxi on the busy street.
Hailing a taxi in the rain is difficult.
They hailed a taxi to escape the crowd.
I usually hail a taxi near the station.
She raised her hand to hail a taxi.
It took a long time to hail a taxi.
The tourists hailed a taxi to go sightseeing.
extraExampleTranslations: U gavjum ko‘chada taksi to‘xtatdi.
Yomg‘irda taksi to‘xtatish qiyin.
Ular olomondan qochish uchun taksi to‘xtatishdi.
Men odatda vokzal yaqinida taksi to‘xtataman.
U qo‘lini ko‘tarib taksi to‘xtatdi.
Taksi to‘xtatish uchun ancha vaqt ketdi.
Sayyohlar diqqatga sazovor joylarni ko‘rish uchun taksi to‘xtatishdi.

words.definition: A thin layer of dust covering a surface.
words.definitionUz: Yupqa chang qatlami — biror yuzani qoplagan nozik chang qatlami.
words.example1: There was a film of dust on the old books.
words.example1Translation: Eski kitoblar ustida yupqa chang qatlami bor edi.
words.example2: She wiped the film of dust from the table.
words.example2Translation: U stoldagi yupqa chang qatlamini artib tashladi.
words.example3: A film of dust covered the furniture.
words.example3Translation: Mebellarni yupqa chang qatlami qoplagan edi.
extraExamples: The car had a film of dust after the long trip.
A thin film of dust settled on the windows.
He cleaned the film of dust from the shelves.
The film of dust made everything look old.
There was only a light film of dust.
The room had a noticeable film of dust.
She removed the film of dust carefully.
extraExampleTranslations: Uzoq safardan keyin mashinada yupqa chang qatlami paydo bo‘lgan edi.
Derazalarga nozik chang qatlami cho‘kkan.
U javonlardagi chang qatlamini tozaladi.
Yupqa chang qatlami hamma narsani eski ko‘rsatardi.
Faqat engil chang qatlami bor edi.
Xonada sezilarli chang qatlami bor edi.
U chang qatlamini ehtiyotkorlik bilan olib tashladi.

words.definition: To relax and enjoy yourself without worrying about rules or appearances.
words.definitionUz: Bo‘shashmoq — qoidalardan yoki tashqi ko‘rinishdan xavotirlanmasdan dam olish va zavqlanish.
words.example1: It’s time to let your hair down and have some fun.
words.example1Translation: Bo‘shashib, biroz zavqlanish vaqti keldi.
words.example2: She finally let her hair down at the party.
words.example2Translation: U ziyofatda nihoyat bo‘shashdi.
words.example3: After work, he likes to let his hair down.
words.example3Translation: Ishdan keyin u bo‘shashishni yaxshi ko‘radi.
extraExamples: Come on, let your hair down for once!
The team let their hair down after the big win.
It’s healthy to let your hair down sometimes.
She doesn’t often let her hair down.
Letting your hair down helps reduce stress.
At the beach, everyone let their hair down.
He finally let his hair down and danced.
extraExampleTranslations: Qani, bir marta bo‘lsa ham bo‘shashing!
Katta g‘alabadan keyin jamoa bo‘shashdi.
Ba’zida bo‘shashish sog‘lom holat.
U kamdan-kam bo‘shashadi.
Bo‘shashish stressni kamaytirishga yordam beradi.
Plyajda hamma bo‘shashgan edi.
U nihoyat bo‘shashib, raqsga tushdi.

words.definition: To begin something badly or in an unfavourable way.
words.definitionUz: Noto‘g‘ri boshlash — biror ishni yomon yoki noqulay tarzda boshlash.
words.example1: They started on the wrong foot and argued immediately.
words.example1Translation: Ular noto‘g‘ri boshlab, darhol bahslashishdi.
words.example2: I started the new job on the wrong foot by being late.
words.example2Translation: Yangi ishni kechikib kelib noto‘g‘ri boshladim.
words.example3: The meeting started on the wrong foot.
words.example3Translation: Yig‘ilish noto‘g‘ri boshlandi.
extraExamples: The couple started their marriage on the wrong foot.
Don’t start on the wrong foot with your new boss.
The project started on the wrong foot due to poor planning.
We started on the wrong foot but became good friends.
The team started the season on the wrong foot.
She realized she had started on the wrong foot.
It’s better to avoid starting on the wrong foot.
extraExampleTranslations: Er-xotin oilaviy hayotni noto‘g‘ri boshlashdi.
Yangi rahbaringiz bilan noto‘g‘ri boshlamang.
Loyiha yomon rejalashtirilgani uchun noto‘g‘ri boshlandi.
Biz noto‘g‘ri boshlagan edik, lekin yaxshi do‘st bo‘lib qoldik.
Jamoa mavsumni noto‘g‘ri boshladi.
U noto‘g‘ri boshlaganini anglab yetdi.
Noto‘g‘ri boshlashdan qochgan yaxshi.

words.definition: As a result of someone’s actions, often harmful or negative.
words.definitionUz: ... qo‘lidan — kimningdir harakatlari natijasida (ko‘pincha salbiy yoki zarar yetkazuvchi).
words.example1: Many civilians died at the hands of the invaders.
words.example1Translation: Ko‘plab tinch aholi bosqinchilar qo‘lidan halok bo‘ldi.
words.example2: He suffered greatly at the hands of his enemies.
words.example2Translation: U dushmanlari qo‘lidan katta azob chekdi.
words.example3: The team lost at the hands of a weaker opponent.
words.example3Translation: Jamoa kuchsizroq raqib qo‘lidan mag‘lub bo‘ldi.
extraExamples: The country suffered at the hands of corrupt leaders.
She was humiliated at the hands of her colleagues.
Many animals have suffered at the hands of humans.
The company collapsed at the hands of bad management.
He met his end at the hands of a rival.
The town was destroyed at the hands of the storm.
Justice was finally served at the hands of the court.
extraExampleTranslations: Mamlakat korrupsioner rahbarlar qo‘lidan azob chekdi.
U hamkasblari qo‘lidan tahqirlandi.
Ko‘plab hayvonlar inson qo‘lidan azob chekkan.
Kompaniya yomon boshqaruv qo‘lidan quladi.
U raqibi qo‘lidan halok bo‘ldi.
Shahar bo‘ron qo‘lidan vayron bo‘ldi.
Adolat nihoyat sud qo‘lidan o‘z o‘rnini topdi.

words.definition: To identify or understand exactly what the problem or situation is.
words.definitionUz: Aniq sababini topmoq — muammo yoki vaziyatning aniq sababini yoki mohiyatini aniqlash.
words.example1: I can’t quite put my finger on what’s wrong.
words.example1Translation: Nima noto‘g‘ri ekanligini aniq tushuna olmayapman.
words.example2: She put her finger on the real problem immediately.
words.example2Translation: U haqiqiy muammoni darhol aniqladi.
words.example3: Can you put your finger on why the project failed?
words.example3Translation: Loyiha nega muvaffaqiyatsiz bo‘lganini aniqlay olasizmi?
extraExamples: I can’t put my finger on it, but something feels off.
The detective put his finger on the motive.
She quickly put her finger on the issue.
It’s hard to put your finger on the exact cause.
He put his finger on the key difference.
No one could put their finger on the problem.
The expert put his finger on the solution.
extraExampleTranslations: Aniq tushuna olmayapman, lekin biror narsa noto‘g‘ri tuyulmoqda.
Tergovchi sababni aniqladi.
U muammoni tezda aniqladi.
Aniq sababni topish qiyin.
U asosiy farqni aniqladi.
Hech kim muammoni aniqlay olmadi.
Mutaxassis yechimni aniqladi.

words.definition: To gain control or an advantage over someone or something.
words.definitionUz: Ustunlikka ega bo‘lmoq — kimdir yoki biror narsa ustidan nazorat yoki ustunlikka erishish.
words.example1: Our team got the upper hand in the second half.
words.example1Translation: Jamoamiz ikkinchi bo‘limda ustunlikka ega bo‘ldi.
words.example2: She managed to get the upper hand in the negotiation.
words.example2Translation: U muzokaralarda ustunlikka erishdi.
words.example3: The company is trying to get the upper hand in the market.
words.example3Translation: Kompaniya bozorda ustunlikka erishishga harakat qilmoqda.
extraExamples: The police got the upper hand over the criminals.
He always tries to get the upper hand in arguments.
Getting the upper hand early is important.
The home team got the upper hand quickly.
She got the upper hand by working harder.
The virus got the upper hand during winter.
We need a strategy to get the upper hand.
extraExampleTranslations: Politsiya jinoyatchilar ustidan ustunlikka erishdi.
U bahslarda har doim ustunlikka erishishga harakat qiladi.
Erta ustunlikka erishish muhim.
Uy jamoasi tezda ustunlikka erishdi.
U qattiqroq ishlab ustunlikka erishdi.
Qishda virus ustunlikka erishdi.
Ustunlikka erishish uchun strategiya kerak.

words.definition: To take a reckless risk that is likely to have serious or dangerous consequences.
words.definitionUz: Olov bilan o‘ynamoq – jiddiy xavf tug‘diradigan tavakkal qilish, oqibati og‘ir bo‘lishi mumkin bo‘lgan ishga qo‘l urish.
words.example1: Skipping safety checks is playing with fire.
words.example1Translation: Xavfsizlik tekshiruvlarini o‘tkazmaslik – olov bilan o‘ynashdir.
words.example2: He knew he was playing with fire by investing all his savings.
words.example2Translation: Barcha jamg‘armasini investitsiya qilish bilan u olov bilan o‘ynayotganini bilardi.
words.example3: Criticizing the boss in public is playing with fire.
words.example3Translation: Rahbarni ochiqdan-ochiq tanqid qilish – olov bilan o‘ynashdir.
extraExamples: Sharing confidential information is playing with fire.
She warned him not to play with fire by lying again.
Playing with fire can destroy your career.
Some people enjoy playing with fire for the thrill.
Ignoring health warnings is playing with fire.
The politician was playing with fire with his controversial statements.
It’s better not to play with fire in business deals.
extraExampleTranslations: Maxfiy ma’lumotni baham ko‘rish – olov bilan o‘ynashdir.
U unga yana yolg‘on gapirib olov bilan o‘ynashmaslikni ogohlantirdi.
Olov bilan o‘ynash karyerangizni yo‘q qilishi mumkin.
Ba’zi odamlar hayajon uchun olov bilan o‘ynashni yoqtirishadi.
Sog‘liq haqidagi ogohlantirishlarga e’tibor bermaslik – olov bilan o‘ynashdir.
Siyosatchi bahsli gaplari bilan olov bilan o‘ynayotgan edi.
Biznes bitimlarida olov bilan o‘ynamaslik yaxshiroq.

words.definition: To strike a surface forcefully with your fist to show anger or emphasize a point.
words.definitionUz: Mushtingizni urmoq – g‘azabni bildirish yoki biror fikrni ta’kidlash uchun qo‘lingiz bilan kuchli urish.
words.example1: He banged his fist on the table in frustration.
words.example1Translation: U g‘azabdan stolga mushti bilan urdi.
words.example2: The coach banged his fist to motivate the players.
words.example2Translation: Murabbiy o‘yinchilarni ruhlantirish uchun mushti bilan urdi.
words.example3: She banged her fist angrily during the argument.
words.example3Translation: U bahs paytida g‘azab bilan mushti bilan urdi.
extraExamples: The judge banged his fist for order in the court.
Banging your fist rarely solves problems.
He banged his fist on the wall out of anger.
The speaker banged his fist to stress his point.
Banging your fist shows strong emotion.
Don’t bang your fist in public – it looks aggressive.
She banged her fist in excitement after the win.
extraExampleTranslations: Sudya tartib o‘rnatish uchun mushti bilan stolga urdi.
Mushtingizni urish muammolarni hal qilmaydi.
U g‘azabdan devorga mushti bilan urdi.
Notiq o‘z fikrini ta’kidlash uchun mushti bilan urdi.
Mushtingizni urish kuchli his-tuyg‘uni ko‘rsatadi.
Ommaviy joyda mushtingizni urmang – tajovuzkor ko‘rinadi.
U g‘alabadan keyin hayajonda mushti bilan urdi.

words.word: Take the law into your own hands
words.pronunciation: /teɪk ðə lɔː ˈɪntu jɔː(r) əʊn hændz/
words.definition: To act independently of legal authority, often in a violent or illegal way to punish someone.
words.definitionUz: O‘zingiz qonunni qo‘lingizga olmoq – qonun organlarisiz, ko‘pincha zo‘ravonlik yoki noqonuniy tarzda o‘zingiz adolat o‘rnatishga urinmoq.
words.example1: You can’t just take the law into your own hands.
words.example1Translation: Siz shunchaki qonunni o‘z qo‘lingizga ola olmaysiz.
words.example2: He took the law into his own hands and attacked the thief.
words.example2Translation: U qonunni o‘z qo‘liga olib, o‘g‘rini hujum qildi.
words.example3: Taking the law into your own hands often leads to more problems.
words.example3Translation: Qonunni o‘z qo‘lingizga olish ko‘pincha yana ko‘proq muammolarga olib keladi.
extraExamples: Vigilantes take the law into their own hands.
The community was accused of taking the law into their own hands.
It’s dangerous to take the law into your own hands.
He regretted taking the law into his own hands.
The police warned people not to take the law into their own hands.
Taking the law into your own hands undermines the justice system.
In movies, heroes often take the law into their own hands.
extraExampleTranslations: O‘zini o‘zi himoya qiluvchilar qonunni o‘z qo‘liga oladi.
Jamiyat qonunni o‘z qo‘liga olganlikda ayblandi.
Qonunni o‘z qo‘lingizga olish xavfli.
U qonunni o‘z qo‘liga olganidan afsuslandi.
Politsiya odamlarga qonunni o‘z qo‘liga olmaslikni ogohlantirdi.
Qonunni o‘z qo‘lingizga olish adliya tizimini zaiflashtiradi.
Kinofilmlarda qahramonlar ko‘pincha qonunni o‘z qo‘liga oladi.

words.definition: To act according to your own judgment and what you think is best.
words.definitionUz: O‘zingizga ma’qul ko‘rganicha qiling – o‘zingizning fikringiz va eng to‘g‘ri deb hisoblaganingiz bo‘yicha harakat qilish.
words.example1: You can do as you see fit with the remaining budget.
words.example1Translation: Qolgan byudjet bilan o‘zingizga ma’qul ko‘rganicha qiling.
words.example2: I trust you – do as you see fit.
words.example2Translation: Sizga ishonaman – o‘zingizga ma’qul ko‘rganicha qiling.
words.example3: The manager told the team to do as they saw fit.
words.example3Translation: Rahbar jamoaga o‘zlari ma’qul ko‘rganicha qilishlarini aytdi.
extraExamples: Handle the situation as you see fit.
Do as you see fit, but be careful.
She decided to do as she saw fit in her personal life.
The teacher allowed students to do as they saw fit for the project.
You’re in charge – do as you see fit.
He did as he saw fit and ignored the advice.
Sometimes you have to do as you see fit.
extraExampleTranslations: Vaziyatni o‘zingizga ma’qul ko‘rganicha hal qiling.
O‘zingizga ma’qul ko‘rganicha qiling, lekin ehtiyot bo‘ling.
U shaxsiy hayotida o‘ziga ma’qul ko‘rganicha qilishga qaror qildi.
O‘qituvchi talabalarga loyiha ustida o‘zlari ma’qul ko‘rganicha ishlashga ruxsat berdi.
Siz mas’ulsiz – o‘zingizga ma’qul ko‘rganicha qiling.
U maslahatni e’tiborga olmasdan o‘ziga ma’qul ko‘rganicha qildi.
Ba’zida o‘zingizga ma’qul ko‘rganicha qilish kerak.

words.definition: To rest and relax, usually by sitting or lying down with your feet raised.
words.definitionUz: Oyoqlaringizni ko‘tarib dam olmoq – oyoqlaringizni yuqoriga ko‘tarib o‘tirib yoki yotib tinchlanish, dam olish.
words.example1: After a long day, I just want to put my feet up.
words.example1Translation: Uzun kunlik mehnatdan keyin faqat oyoqlarimni ko‘tarib dam olmoqchiman.
words.example2: Put your feet up and watch a movie.
words.example2Translation: Oyoqlaringizni ko‘tarib film tomosha qiling.
words.example3: She put her feet up with a cup of tea.
words.example3Translation: U choy ichib, oyoqlarini ko‘tarib dam oldi.
extraExamples: It’s nice to put your feet up after work.
Grandpa loves to put his feet up in the evening.
Put your feet up – you deserve a rest.
She rarely gets time to put her feet up.
Putting your feet up helps reduce stress.
He put his feet up and fell asleep quickly.
During the holiday, we just put our feet up.
extraExampleTranslations: Ishdan keyin oyoqni ko‘tarib dam olish juda yoqimli.
Bobom kechqurun oyoqlarini ko‘tarib o‘tirishni yaxshi ko‘radi.
Oyoqlaringizni ko‘tarib dam oling – siz dam olishga loyiqsiz.
U kamdan-kam oyoqlarini ko‘tarib dam olishga vaqt topadi.
Oyoqni ko‘tarib dam olish stressni kamaytiradi.
U oyoqlarini ko‘tarib, tez uxlab qoldi.
Dam olish kunlarida biz faqat oyoqni ko‘tarib dam oldik.

words.definition: To deal with or manage a situation or problem competently.
words.definitionUz: Boshqarmoq / hal qilmoq – biror vaziyat yoki muammoni muvaffaqiyatli boshqarish yoki hal etish.
words.example1: She handled the difficult customer very well.
words.example1Translation: U qiyin mijozni juda yaxshi boshqardi.
words.example2: The manager handled the crisis calmly.
words.example2Translation: Rahbar inqirozni tinchlik bilan hal qildi.
words.example3: Can you handle this project on your own?
words.example3Translation: Bu loyihani o‘zingiz hal qila olasizmi?
extraExamples: He handled the situation with great skill.
The doctor handled the emergency professionally.
Learning to handle stress is important.
She can handle multiple tasks at once.
The police handled the protest peacefully.
I don’t know how to handle this problem.
He handled the bad news with dignity.
extraExampleTranslations: U vaziyatni katta mahorat bilan boshqardi.
Shifokor favqulodda holatni professional tarzda hal qildi.
Stressni boshqarishni o‘rganish muhim.
U bir vaqtning o‘zida bir nechta vazifani boshqara oladi.
Politsiya namoyishni tinch yo‘l bilan boshqardi.
Bu muammoni qanday hal qilishni bilmayman.
U yomon xabarni hurmat bilan qabul qildi.

words.definition: To perform an action stylishly, creatively, and with confidence.
words.definitionUz: Uslub bilan qilmoq – biror ishni chiroyli, ijodiy va o‘ziga ishonch bilan bajarish.
words.example1: She always does her presentations with flair.
words.example1Translation: U har doim taqdimotlarini uslub bilan o‘tkazadi.
words.example2: The chef cooked the meal with flair.
words.example2Translation: Oshpaz taomni uslub bilan tayyorladi.
words.example3: He solved the problem with flair and creativity.
words.example3Translation: U muammoni uslub va ijodkorlik bilan hal qildi.
extraExamples: The dancer performed with great flair.
Do everything with flair and passion.
She decorated the room with flair.
The teacher explained the lesson with flair.
He dresses with flair.
The writer tells stories with flair.
Doing your job with flair makes it more enjoyable.
extraExampleTranslations: Raqqosa katta uslub bilan ijro etdi.
Hamma narsani uslub va ishtiyoq bilan bajaring.
U xonani uslub bilan bezadi.
O‘qituvchi darsni uslub bilan tushuntirdi.
U kiyinishda uslubga ega.
Yozuvchi hikoyalarni uslub bilan aytadi.
Ishni uslub bilan bajarish uni yanada yoqimli qiladi.

words.definition: A large and impressive financial gain.
words.definitionUz: Yaxshi foyda – katta va ta’sirli moliyaviy daromad.
words.example1: The company made a handsome profit this year.
words.example1Translation: Kompaniya bu yili yaxshi foyda ko‘rdi.
words.example2: He sold the house for a handsome profit.
words.example2Translation: U uyni yaxshi foyda bilan sotdi.
words.example3: Investing early can lead to a handsome profit.
words.example3Translation: Erta investitsiya qilish yaxshi foydaga olib kelishi mumkin.
extraExamples: The business yielded a handsome profit.
She made a handsome profit from selling her paintings.
A handsome profit motivated the team.
They earned a handsome profit from the deal.
Turning the old building into a hotel brought a handsome profit.
Making a handsome profit isn’t everything.
The startup hopes for a handsome profit soon.
extraExampleTranslations: Biznes yaxshi foyda keltirdi.
U rasmlarini sotib yaxshi foyda oldi.
Yaxshi foyda jamoani rag‘batlantirdi.
Ular bitimdan yaxshi foyda ko‘rishdi.
Eski binoni mehmonxonaga aylantirish yaxshi foyda keltirdi.
Yaxshi foyda ko‘rish hammasi emas.
Startap yaqin orada yaxshi foyda ko‘rishga umid qilmoqda.

words.definition: To catch fire suddenly and violently.
words.definitionUz: To‘satdan yonib ketmoq – birdan kuchli olov chiqib yonib ketish.
words.example1: The car burst into flames after the crash.
words.example1Translation: Avariyadan keyin mashina to‘satdan yonib ketdi.
words.example2: The dry grass burst into flames quickly.
words.example2Translation: Quruq o‘t tezda yonib ketdi.
words.example3: The building burst into flames within minutes.
words.example3Translation: Bino bir necha daqiqada yonib ketdi.
extraExamples: The fireworks burst into flames in the sky.
Oil can burst into flames if not handled carefully.
The old curtains burst into flames.
The engine suddenly burst into flames.
A spark caused the forest to burst into flames.
The cake burst into flames when the candles were lit.
Don’t let the situation burst into flames.
extraExampleTranslations: Otashinlar osmonda yonib ketdi.
Yog‘ ehtiyotkorlik bilan ishlatilmasa, to‘satdan yonib ketishi mumkin.
Eski pardalar birdan yonib ketdi.
Dvigatel to‘satdan yonib ketdi.
Uchqun o‘rmonni yonib ketishiga sabab bo‘ldi.
Shamlar yoqilganda tort yonib ketdi.
Vaziyatni yonib ketishiga yo‘l qo‘ymang.

words.definition: To prove useful when needed.
words.definitionUz: Foydali bo‘lmoq – kerak bo‘lganda yordam beradigan, foydali chiqadigan narsa.
words.example1: This extra tool will come in handy later.
words.example1Translation: Bu qo‘shimcha asbob keyinroq foydali bo‘ladi.
words.example2: Knowing a second language always comes in handy.
words.example2Translation: Ikkinchi tilni bilish har doim foydali bo‘ladi.
words.example3: The map came in handy during our trip.
words.example3Translation: Xarita sayohatimizda juda foydali bo‘ldi.
extraExamples: Old notebooks can come in handy for ideas.
Learning basic repairs will come in handy.
Her experience came in handy during the crisis.
A good sense of humor always comes in handy.
This advice will come in handy someday.
Extra money always comes in handy.
The flashlight came in handy when the power went out.
extraExampleTranslations: Eski daftarlar g‘oyalar uchun foydali bo‘lishi mumkin.
Asosiy ta’mirlashni o‘rganish foydali bo‘ladi.
Uning tajribasi inqiroz paytida foydali bo‘ldi.
Yaxshi hazil tuyg‘usi har doim foydali.
Bu maslahat bir kun foydali bo‘ladi.
Qo‘shimcha pul har doim kerak bo‘ladi.
Chiroq elektr o‘chganda foydali bo‘ldi.

words.definition: To appear suddenly on a screen or surface.
words.definitionUz: To‘satdan paydo bo‘lmoq – ekran yoki yuzada birdan paydo bo‘lish (xabar, tasvir va h.k.).
words.example1: A warning message flashed up on the screen.
words.example1Translation: Ekranda ogohlantirish xabari to‘satdan paydo bo‘ldi.
words.example2: Her name flashed up on the leaderboard.
words.example2Translation: Unisning ismi yetakchilar jadvalida to‘satdan paydo bo‘ldi.
words.example3: The news alert flashed up on my phone.
words.example3Translation: Telefonimda yangilik bildirishi to‘satdan chiqdi.
extraExamples: An error flashed up on the computer.
Pictures of the event flashed up on the big screen.
The score flashed up at the end of the match.
A notification flashed up while I was working.
Memories flashed up in her mind.
The advertisement flashed up suddenly.
Important updates will flash up on the app.
extraExampleTranslations: Kompyuterda xato xabari to‘satdan paydo bo‘ldi.
Voqea rasmlari katta ekranda to‘satdan chiqdi.
O‘yin oxirida hisob to‘satdan paydo bo‘ldi.
Ishlayotganimda bildirishnoma to‘satdan chiqdi.
Xotiralar uning xayolida to‘satdan paydo bo‘ldi.
Reklama birdan paydo bo‘ldi.
Muhim yangilanishlar ilovada to‘satdan chiqadi.

words.definition: To gain knowledge or understanding through unpleasant or difficult experiences.
words.definitionUz: Og‘ir tajriba bilan o‘rganmoq – qiyin yoki yoqimsiz voqealar orqali bilim olish.
words.example1: I learned the hard way not to trust everyone.
words.example1Translation: Hamma odamga ishonmaslikni men og‘ir tajriba bilan o‘rgandim.
words.example2: He learned the hard way that preparation is important.
words.example2Translation: Tayyorgarlik muhimligini u og‘ir tajriba bilan o‘rgandi.
words.example3: Many people learn the hard way about financial management.
words.example3Translation: Ko‘p odamlar moliyaviy boshqaruvni og‘ir tajriba bilan o‘rganishadi.
extraExamples: She learned the hard way not to leave her phone unlocked.
We all learn the hard way sometimes.
He learned the hard way after losing his job.
Learning the hard way can be painful but effective.
Don’t wait to learn the hard way.
The team learned the hard way about teamwork.
Children often learn the hard way.
extraExampleTranslations: U telefonini qulflamaslikni og‘ir tajriba bilan o‘rgandi.
Biz hammamiz ba’zida og‘ir tajriba bilan o‘rganamiz.
U ishsiz qolgandan keyin og‘ir tajriba bilan o‘rgandi.
Og‘ir tajriba bilan o‘rganish og‘riqli, lekin samarali.
Og‘ir tajriba bilan o‘rganishni kutib turmang.
Jamoa jamoaviy ish haqida og‘ir tajriba bilan o‘rgandi.
Bolalar ko‘pincha og‘ir tajriba bilan o‘rganishadi.

words.definition: To manage to survive financially or emotionally in a difficult situation.
words.definitionUz: Boshi chiqib turmoq – qiyin moliyaviy yoki ruhiy vaziyatda omon qolish, boshini suvdan chiqarib yurish.
words.example1: With rising prices, many families are struggling to keep their heads above water.
words.example1Translation: Narxlar oshgani sababli ko‘plab oilalar boshini suvdan chiqarib yurishga qiynalmoqda.
words.example2: I’m just trying to keep my head above water at work.
words.example2Translation: Ishda faqat boshimni suvdan chiqarib yurishga urinayapman.
words.example3: She kept her head above water during the difficult period.
words.example3Translation: U qiyin davrda boshini suvdan chiqarib yurdi.
extraExamples: Small businesses are fighting to keep their heads above water.
It’s hard to keep your head above water with so much debt.
He is barely keeping his head above water emotionally.
The scholarship helped her keep her head above water.
We need to work together to keep our heads above water.
Keeping your head above water is the first priority right now.
Many students struggle to keep their heads above water with studies and jobs.
extraExampleTranslations: Kichik bizneslar boshini suvdan chiqarib yurish uchun kurashmoqda.
Buncha qarz bilan boshni suvdan chiqarib yurish qiyin.
U ruhiy jihatdan zo‘rg‘a boshini suvdan chiqarib yuribdi.
Stipendiya unga boshini suvdan chiqarib yurishga yordam berdi.
Boshimizni suvdan chiqarib yurish uchun birgalikda ishlashimiz kerak.
Hozir boshni suvdan chiqarib yurish birinchi ustuvorlik.
Ko‘plab talabalar o‘qish va ish bilan boshlarini suvdan chiqarib yurishga qiynalishadi.

words.definition: Seriously imperfect or having fundamental problems.
words.definitionUz: Jiddiy nuqsonli – asosiy jihatdan muammoli yoki jiddiy kamchiliklarga ega.
words.example1: The plan was deeply flawed from the beginning.
words.example1Translation: Reja boshidanoq jiddiy nuqsonli edi.
words.example2: His argument is deeply flawed.
words.example2Translation: Uning dalili jiddiy nuqsonli.
words.example3: The system is deeply flawed and needs reform.
words.example3Translation: Tizim jiddiy nuqsonli va isloh talab qiladi.
extraExamples: The research was deeply flawed.
A deeply flawed character can be interesting in stories.
The law is deeply flawed in its current form.
Her understanding of the topic was deeply flawed.
The deeply flawed product was recalled.
Society is deeply flawed but improving.
The deeply flawed decision cost the company millions.
extraExampleTranslations: Tadqiqot jiddiy nuqsonli edi.
Jiddiy nuqsonli qahramon hikoyalarda qiziqarli bo‘lishi mumkin.
Qonun hozirgi shaklida jiddiy nuqsonli.
Uning mavzuni tushunishi jiddiy nuqsonli edi.
Jiddiy nuqsonli mahsulot qaytarib olindi.
Jamiyat jiddiy nuqsonli, lekin yaxshilanmoqda.
Jiddiy nuqsonli qaror kompaniyaga millionlab zarar keltirdi.

words.definition: Despite; even though (used to show contrast).
words.definitionUz: Barchasiga qaramay – biror narsaga qaramay, shunga qaramasdan (qarama-qarshilik bildirish uchun).
words.example1: For all his efforts, he still failed the exam.
words.example1Translation: Barcha harakatlariga qaramay, u imtihondan o‘ta olmadi.
words.example2: For all her talent, she remains humble.
words.example2Translation: Barcha iste’dodiga qaramay, u kamtar bo‘lib qoldi.
words.example3: For all the criticism, the movie was quite successful.
words.example3Translation: Barcha tanqidlarga qaramay, film ancha muvaffaqiyatli bo‘ldi.
extraExamples: For all his wealth, he is not happy.
For all their promises, nothing changed.
For all the rain, the event was well attended.
For all her experience, she made a beginner mistake.
For all the hype, the product was disappointing.
For all his faults, he is a good friend.
For all our planning, things still went wrong.
extraExampleTranslations: Barcha boyligiga qaramay, u baxtli emas.
Barcha va’dalariga qaramay, hech narsa o‘zgarmadi.
Yomg‘irga qaramay, tadbirga ko‘p odam keldi.
Barcha tajribasiga qaramay, u yangi boshlovchi xatosini qildi.
Barcha shov-shuvga qaramay, mahsulot umidsizlikka tushirdi.
Barcha kamchiliklariga qaramay, u yaxshi do‘st.
Barcha rejalashlarimizga qaramay, ishlar baribir noto‘g‘ri ketdi.

words.definition: To laugh uncontrollably and very loudly.
words.definitionUz: Qotib kulmoq – o‘zini tutolmay, juda baland va uzoq vaqt kulish.
words.example1: The comedy show made us laugh our heads off.
words.example1Translation: Komediya shousi bizni qotib kuldirib yubordi.
words.example2: We laughed our heads off at his jokes.
words.example2Translation: Uning hazillaridan qotib kuldik.
words.example3: The children were laughing their heads off at the cartoon.
words.example3Translation: Bolalar multfilmni ko‘rib qotib kulishdi.
extraExamples: The video made everyone laugh their heads off.
I laughed my head off when I saw the photo.
They laughed their heads off for ten minutes straight.
Laughing your head off is the best medicine.
The whole class was laughing their heads off.
She laughed her head off at the silly mistake.
We all laughed our heads off remembering old stories.
extraExampleTranslations: Video hammani qotib kuldirib yubordi.
Rasmni ko‘rib qotib kuldim.
Ular o‘n daqiqa davomida qotib kulishdi.
Qotib kulish eng yaxshi dori.
Butun sinf qotib kulardi.
U ahmoqona xatodan qotib kulgandi.
Eski hikoyalarni eslab hammamiz qotib kuldik.

words.definition: To look quickly through the pages of a book, magazine, or content.
words.definitionUz: Tez varaqlab ko‘rmoq – kitob, jurnal yoki kontentni sahifalarni tez-tez varaqlab, yuzaki ko‘rib chiqish.
words.example1: She flicked through the magazine while waiting.
words.example1Translation: U kutayotganda jurnalni tez varaqlab ko‘rdi.
words.example2: I flicked through the report before the meeting.
words.example2Translation: Uchrashuv oldidan hisobotni tez varaqlab ko‘rdim.
words.example3: He flicked through the photos on his phone.
words.example3Translation: U telefonidagi rasmlarni tez varaqlab ko‘rdi.
extraExamples: Flick through the book to get an idea.
She flicked through the channels on TV.
I like to flick through old yearbooks.
Flicking through social media takes a lot of time.
He flicked through the newspaper quickly.
Students often flick through textbooks before exams.
Flick through the menu and choose something.
extraExampleTranslations: Kitobni tez varaqlab umumiy tasavvur hosil qiling.
U televizorda kanallarni tez varaqladi.
Men eski yillik jurnallarni varaqlashni yoqtiraman.
Ijtimoiy tarmoqlarni varaqlash ko‘p vaqt oladi.
U gazetani tez varaqlab chiqdi.
Talabalar imtihon oldidan darsliklarni tez-tez varaqlab chiqishadi.
Menyuni tez varaqlab biror narsa tanlang.

words.definition: The results or rewards of effort or work.
words.definitionUz: … mevalari – mehnat yoki harakatning natijalari yoki mukofotlari.
words.example1: She is now enjoying the fruits of her hard work.
words.example1Translation: U endi o‘z mehnatining mevalaridan bahramand bo‘lmoqda.
words.example2: The fruits of their research changed the industry.
words.example2Translation: Ular tadqiqotining mevalari sanoatni o‘zgartirdi.
words.example3: Patience brings the fruits of success.
words.example3Translation: Sabr muvaffaqiyat mevalarini keltiradi.
extraExamples: We are reaping the fruits of our investment.
The fruits of education last a lifetime.
He sacrificed everything for the fruits of fame.
The team is proud of the fruits of their labor.
The fruits of innovation benefit everyone.
Enjoy the fruits of your success.
The fruits of peace are prosperity and happiness.
extraExampleTranslations: Biz investitsiyamizning mevalarini yig‘ib olyapmiz.
Ta’lim mevalari umr bo‘yi davom etadi.
U shuhrat mevalari uchun hamma narsani qurbon qildi.
Jamoa mehnatlarining mevalari bilan faxrlanadi.
Innovatsiya mevalari hammaga foyda keltiradi.
Muvaffaqiyatingiz mevalaridan bahramand bo‘ling.
Tinchlikning mevalari farovonlik va baxtdir.

words.word: Have a roof over your head
words.definition: To have a place to live; basic shelter.
words.definitionUz: Boshpana bo‘lmoq – yashash uchun joyga ega bo‘lish, tom ostida turish.
words.example1: At least we have a roof over our heads.
words.example1Translation: Hech bo‘lmaganda boshimizda tom bor.
words.example2: Many families don’t even have a roof over their heads.
words.example2Translation: Ko‘plab oilalarning hatto boshpanasi ham yo‘q.
words.example3: Having a roof over your head is a basic human need.
words.example3Translation: Boshpanaga ega bo‘lish insonning asosiy ehtiyojidir.
extraExamples: I’m grateful just to have a roof over my head.
The shelter provides a roof over people’s heads.
He worked hard to put a roof over his family’s heads.
Having a roof over your head gives a sense of security.
Many refugees are still without a roof over their heads.
A simple tent was better than no roof over their heads.
The charity aims to give everyone a roof over their heads.
extraExampleTranslations: Faqat boshimda tom borligi uchun shukr qilaman.
Boshpana odamlarga tom ostida joy beradi.
U oilasiga boshpana berish uchun qattiq mehnat qildi.
Boshpanaga ega bo‘lish xavfsizlik hissi beradi.
Ko‘plab qochqinlar hali ham boshpanasiz.
Oddiy chodir ham hech qanday tom ostida bo‘lmagandan yaxshi edi.
Xayriya tashkiloti har kimga boshpana berishni maqsad qilgan.

words.definition: To receive a very large number of things (calls, messages, offers, etc.) at once.
words.definitionUz: To‘lib ketmoq – bir vaqtning o‘zida juda ko‘p narsa (qo‘ng‘iroq, xabar, taklif va h.k.) kelib tushishi.
words.example1: The company was flooded with orders after the advertisement.
words.example1Translation: Reklamadan keyin kompaniya buyurtmalar bilan to‘lib ketdi.
words.example2: Her phone was flooded with messages of support.
words.example2Translation: Telefoniga qo‘llab-quvvatlash xabarlari bilan to‘lib ketdi.
words.example3: The website was flooded with visitors.
words.example3Translation: Veb-sayt mehmonlar bilan to‘lib ketdi.
extraExamples: The helpline was flooded with calls.
Social media was flooded with reactions.
The school was flooded with applications.
He was flooded with job offers.
The market was flooded with cheap products.
After the news, the studio was flooded with requests.
The city was flooded with tourists during the festival.
extraExampleTranslations: Yordam telefon liniyasi qo‘ng‘iroqlar bilan to‘lib ketdi.
Ijtimoiy tarmoqlar reaktsiyalar bilan to‘lib ketdi.
Maktabga arizalar bilan to‘lib ketdi.
